subject: Confirm your subscription to the ASCE list
body:

Your request to subscribe to the ASCE list (ASCE) requires confirmation.

To confirm that you want to subscribe, please click on this link:

Your request  to subscribe  will be canceled  automatically if  you don't
confirm your subscription  within 48h. If you don't want  to subscribe to
this list, then simply discard this message and let the request expire on

subject: You are now subscribed to the CBITS_EVENTS list
body:
Your  are now  subscribed  to  the CBITS_EVENTS  list  (CBITs Events  and
Seminars).

Please save this message for future  reference, especially if this is the
first time you are subscribing to an electronic mailing list. If you ever
need to  leave the list,  then you  will find the  necessary instructions
below. Perhaps  more importantly, saving a  copy of this message  (and of
all future  subscription notices from  other mailing lists) in  a special
mail folder will give you instant access  to the list of mailing lists to
which you are subscribed.  This may prove useful the next  time you go on
vacation and need to leave the lists temporarily to avoid filling up your
mailbox while  you are away.  You should  also save the  welcome messages
from the  list owners  that you  may receive after  subscribing to  a new
list.

To send a message to all of  the people currently subscribed to the list,
just  send mail  to  CBITS_EVENTS@LISTSERV.IT.NORTHWESTERN.EDU, which  is
called the list address.  You must never try to send  any command to that
address,  as  it  would  be  distributed  to  all  the  people  who  have
subscribed. All commands must be sent to
LISTSERV@LISTSERV.IT.NORTHWESTERN.EDU, which is  the LISTSERV address. It
is important to understand the difference between the two.

You may  leave the list at  any time by sending  a "SIGNOFF CBITS_EVENTS"
command  to  LISTSERV@LISTSERV.IT.NORTHWESTERN.EDU.  You  can  also  tell
LISTSERV how you want  it to confirm the receipt of  messages you send to
the list.  To send  yourself a  copy of  your own  messages, send  a "SET
CBITS_EVENTS REPRO" command.  Alternatively, to have LISTSERV  send you a
short  acknowledgement  instead  of  the  entire  message,  send  a  "SET
CBITS_EVENTS   ACK  NOREPRO"   command.   Finally,  you   can  turn   off
acknowledgements  completely with  the "SET  CBITS_EVENTS NOACK  NOREPRO"
command.

Note: For  users with a  valid Northwestern NetID,  subscription settings
and preferences can also be set using the NU Listserv Web Interface at at
https://listserv.it.northwestern.edu.  For  more   information  on  using
commands or on using the Web Interface to set your subscription settings,
see the List Subscriber's Manual at
http://www.lsoft.com/resources/manuals.asp.  For  users without  a  valid
Northwestern NetID, a  list of available e-mail commands can  be found at
www.it.northwestern.edu/listserv/howto.html .

Following  instructions from  the list  owner, your  subscription options
have  been set  to  "NOACK REPRO  MIME" rather  than  the usual  LISTSERV
defaults. For more information about  subscription options, send a "QUERY
CBITS_EVENTS" command to LISTSERV@LISTSERV.IT.NORTHWESTERN.EDU.

Contributions  sent to  this  list are  automatically  archived. You  can
access the list archives at
https://LISTSERV.IT.NORTHWESTERN.EDU/cgi-bin/wa.exe?A0=CBITS_EVENTS.  You
may need to  log in or get a  password for the Web interface  to view the
archives.

This  list is  available  in digest  form.  If you  wish  to receive  the
digested version of the postings,  then issue a "SET CBITS_EVENTS DIGEST"
command.

More information on NU LISTSERV commands  can be found in the NU LISTSERV
reference  card, which  you can  retrieve  by sending  an "INFO  REFCARD"
command to LISTSERV@LISTSERV.IT.NORTHWESTERN.EDU.

subject: Welcome to All Paws In!
body:
Hey all! We are so happy that  you are interested in joining All Paws In!
The following  email will have lots  of information about what  our goals
are and how you can be involved. Let’s get started!

Our goal as a group is to  connect NU with animals in every way possible.
We want to  make the animals’ lives  better and to enrich our  own at the
same time. We work with the Evanston Animal Shelter (2310 Oak Street) and
they are INCREDIBLE.  Transportation to the shelter is tricky  but we are
figuring out a way to make it more accessible (right now the best mode is
the 93 cta  bus there and trying  to hitch a ride  with another volunteer
back to campus). We are not an “official” club yet, but are applying this
week!

Below we  have outlined different positions  that a member can  hold! You
can participate in any of these positions  or just show up to events that
we may have.  Email us back if you  have ANY questions at all  and let us
know if anything below is intriguing to  you so we can get you in contact
with the right people.

Volunteering positions Animal-handling volunteers: this position requires
a 3 hour long  commitment once a week for 6 months  minimum. You can work
with cats or dogs on your shift and there’s a multitude of duties ranging
from walking dogs to cleaning cages to doing laundry. If you want to work
with animals there are links below so the shelter can approve you and get
in contact with you before starting.  If you do start volunteering, shoot
us an email to keep us in the loop on where our members are.

Dog Volunteer Link https://forms.gle/H7w9LBJSPG8gZiDF7

Cat Volunteer Link https://forms.gle/5xy7ssWxNuJ9GFWr9

Writers: this would be  a sort of “come when you  can” position and won’t
require you  to come  to the shelter.  We would love  to have  someone to
write/rewrite the cutesy blurbs on  the shelter’s website for the animals
and the  shelter needs a  lot of help  with grant writing.  Social Media:
this is another  volunteer position without set hours!  Depending on what
the shelter needs,  your job may change. They want  help with things like
publicity  videos, running  their Instagram,  and  we’d love  to do  some
adorable photoshoots with the dogs.

On-Campus  Needs Event  Planning  and Running:  If  you’re interested  in
planning events such as a dog  bed making event, a socialization fair, or
any ideas you have, join this group.  We have lots of ideas for things to
run on campus and would love  the help. Executive Board (pending new name
because we’re sick  of hearing the word  exec): As of right  now the exec
team will handle the rest of  the responsibilities of the club. If you’re
passionate about what our club  stands for and want leadership experience
and a chance to have an impact then  please let us know and we’ll add you
to a group chat.  Please only take part in this if  you genuinely want to
help out! It’s okay to not be on the exec board for every club <3

Link to groupme: https://groupme.com/join_group/71551566/uNBvQ0M1

Get  to know  your president/founder:  My  name is  JoJo Holm  and I’m  a
sophomore chemistry major! I  live on a farm back home  and love dogs and
all animals  more than anything  ever. I’m  super excited to  be starting
this group and hope  that you all can get everything you  want out of it.
My contact information is below if  you have any questions (or respond to
this email). Phone: (618)979-2567 Email: jojoholm2024@u.northwestern.edu


THANKS FOR READING THIS  FAR IF YOU DID! WE HOPE  TO HAVE ANOTHER MEETING
SOON TO GO OVER ALL THIS INFO AGAIN BUT BETTER!


Hugs, kisses, and belly scratches, All Paws In

Group email (for questions): allpawsinNU@gmail.com

subject: Fwd: 10 Days Left | UPenn x Capital One Case Competition
body:
Warm wishes,
​Jasmine Ong (she/her)
​Northwestern University | Class of 2027
​B.S. ​Industrial Engineering, Minors in Sociology and Science in Human Culture
LinkedIn | (773) 242-5877
---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Consult for America <consultforamerica@10670734.brevosend.com>
Date: Mar 28, 2026 at 10:10 AM +0800
To: jasmineong2027@u.northwestern.edu
Subject: 10 Days Left | UPenn x Capital One Case Competition

> Hello,
>
> I hope you are doing well! I wanted to follow up with a quick reminder that registration for CFA's consulting case competition with Capital One’s Corporate Strategy team closes in just 10 days, on April 6th at 11:59 PM EST. If this interests your club, we hope you could share this opportunity to your members and beyond at Northwestern.
>
> Our case competition will offer:
>
> • > Networking and information sessions providing an invaluable look into Capital One’s Corporate Strategy team
> • > Q&A session with Capital One executives
> • > An opportunity to present your ideas to Capital One's Strategy leaders
> • > Prizes awarded per person:
>     • > 1st Place: $1,000, 2nd Place: $500, 3rd Place: $250
> • > Resume collection for Semi-finalists and Finalists
>
>
> As a reminder, the competition will be held virtually from April 9th to April 20th, 2026. All undergraduate students will be able to register in teams of 3-5. Individual entries are also allowed, and participants will be matched to a team. If any of your club members, or other students at your school, are interested in participating, please register at https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://tinyurl.com/2026capitalone__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!V4qTOCKZv2skvRhCmQFyUnA56McaQBRkhElg1ycq0PrgJTyOPei6W6oyTjmfutYP0nYgVSyoKmO-r_RrEsiLzK2kWxmmk4AhatU7ySrHFL_mKv4$ . More information can be found at consultforamericaupenn.com/case-comp, as well as on the flyer attached below.
> Our theme this year is developing an international growth strategy for Capital One. There is absolutely no experience necessary in either finance or consulting and is a great opportunity to see what consulting is about and flex your creative problem solving skills.
>
> In exchange for sharing the flyer around your school, we would be happy to refer your club as a primary contact to the Capital One Strategy team, providing your members with updated notifications when applications go live!
>
> We look forward to seeing your participation! Please reach back out with any questions.
>
> Best,
> Victor Vasilev and Allison Xu
> External Chairs, Consult for America
> University of Pennsylvania
> If you wish to unsubscribe from our newsletter, click here

########################################################################

To unsubscribe from the CASE list, click the following link:
https://LISTSERV.IT.NORTHWESTERN.EDU/cgi-bin/wa.exe?TICKET=NzM5NzMyIGdyYWNlc2hhbzIwMjZAVS5OT1JUSFdFU1RFUk4uRURVIENBU0UgIKUA5RlxyZ7D&c=SIGNOFF

subject: Next Week's Events at the Roberta Buffett Institute
body:
https://www.buffett.northwestern.edu

https://buffett.northwestern.edu/events/

EDGS BOOK TALK

Bad Lieutenants: Khmer Rouge, United Front, and Class Struggle from 1970-1997 with Andrew Mertha

Wednesday, April 1 | 12:30 p.m.

720 University Place and online

Join the Roberta Buffett Institute's Equality Development and Globalization Studies (EDGS) Program (https://www.edgs.northwestern.edu/) for a public book talk with Andrew Mertha (https://sais.jhu.edu/users/amertha1) , the George and Sadie Hyman Professor of China Studies at the Johns Hopkins University School of Advanced International Studies.

Mertha will present his new book, Bad Lieutenants: The Khmer Rouge, United Front, and Class Struggle, 1970–1997 (https://www.cornellpress.cornell.edu/book/9781501780998/bad-lieutenants/) , which examines how the Khmer Rouge remained a force to be reckoned with even after the fall of Democratic Kampuchea—and of the men behind the movement's durability. Mertha argues that the Khmer Rouge's successes and failures were both driven by a refusal to dilute its revolutionary vision. Rather than take the moderate tack required for viable governance, it pivoted between two political strategies: united front and class struggle. Through the stories of three key leaders—Ieng Sary, Son Sen, and Ta Mok—Mertha tracks the movement's shift from one strategy to the other until its dissolution in the 1990s. Doors will open at 12:00 p.m., and lunch will begin at 12:15 p.m.
REGISTER TO ATTEND IN PERSON (https://www.eventbrite.com/e/edgs-book-talk-bad-lieutenants-with-andrew-mertha-tickets-1849340149169?aff=NextWeeksEvents)
REGISTER TO ATTEND ONLINE (https://northwestern.zoom.us/webinar/register/3217733419042/WN_DK36RRyGRDKYDUFS-XkHWw)

BUFFETT FIRST FRIDAY LUNCH

The Global Health Revolution and Primary Care with Lisa Hirschhorn

Friday, April 3

12:30–1:45 p.m.

720 University Place, Second Floor

Northwestern faculty, students and staff are invited to join us for our April Buffett First Friday Lunch featuring Lisa Hirschhorn (https://www.feinberg.northwestern.edu/faculty-profiles/az/profile.html?xid=36440) , Director of the Ryan Family Center for Global Primary Care (https://www.globalhealth.northwestern.edu/centers/primary-care/index.html) at the Robert J. Havey Institute for Global Health and Professor of Medical Social Sciences at Northwestern University's Feinberg School of Medicine. Professor Hirschhorn will discuss why people-centered primary health care is critical for accelerating and sustaining improvements in global health and population well-being, particularly in an era of limited resources.

Drawing on work supported by the Havey Institute and other grant-funded research, she will review the evidence for primary health care (PHC) as a transformative force in health. She will also highlight the need to redesign PHC to better empower communities and individuals, and outline areas where Northwestern is already engaged—as well as opportunities for additional cross-campus collaboration to strengthen and accelerate progress. Doors will open at 12:00 p.m., and lunch will begin at 12:15 p.m.
REGISTER (https://www.eventbrite.com/e/buffett-first-friday-lunch-the-global-health-revolution-and-primary-care-tickets-1665445234529?aff=NextWeeksEvents)


** LOOKING AHEAD
------------------------------------------------------------

EDGS BOOK TALK

Communication Against Capital: Red Enlightenment at the Dawn of Indonesia with Rianne Subijanto

Monday, April 6 | 12:30–1:30 p.m.

720 University Place and online

Join the Roberta Buffett Institute's Equality Development and Globalization Studies (EDGS) Program (https://www.edgs.northwestern.edu/) for a public book talk with Rianne Subijanto (https://www.baruch.cuny.edu/profiles/faculty/Rianne-Subijanto) , Associate Professor in the Department of Communications Studies at Baruch College and author of Communication against Capital: Red Enlightenment at the Dawn of Indonesia (https://www.cornellpress.cornell.edu/book/9781501778667/communication-against-capital/#bookTabs=1) , which explores the revolutionary communication strategies of the pergerakan merah, the anticolonial "red movement" in 1920s Indonesia. Subijanto tells the story of ordinary lower-class women and children and people of diverse races and ethnicities who waged their battles against Dutch colonialism within multiple arenas of communication, including political associations, assemblies, printed matter, schools, and shipping lines. Doors will open at 12:00 p.m., and lunch will begin at
12:15 p.m.
REGISTER TO ATTEND IN PERSON (https://www.eventbrite.com/e/edgs-book-talk-communication-against-capital-with-rianne-subijanto-tickets-1985737395003?aff=NextWeeksEvents)
REGISTER TO ATTEND ONLINE (https://northwestern.zoom.us/webinar/register/7917713538126/WN_Ic6YHY1yTRWoJsngnlKb5w)

GRADUATE PROGRAMMING

Anthropogenic Pessimism

in Post-Mao Sino-Muslim Fiction with Connie Kang

Tuesday, April 7 | 12:00-1:00 p.m.

720 University Place, Second Floor

The Roberta Buffett Institute for Global Affairs hosts running colloquia for graduate students to present their research to globally engaged faculty and fellow students. In this colloquium, Comparative Literary Studies PhD candidate Connie Kang (https://complit.northwestern.edu/people/graduate-students/kang-connie-kang.html) will present on "Martyrdom as Humanism: Notes Toward an Anthropogenic Pessimism in Post-Mao Sino-Muslim Fiction." Q&A and discussion will follow, and lunch will be provided.

Kang’s dissertation project explores how Sinophone ethnic minority literature renegotiates the relationship between the Chinese Revolution and ethnic and indigenous experiences of revolutionary subjectivation and subjection. This talk examines Hui Muslim author and pro-Palestine activist Zhang Chengzhi’s novel History of the Soul (1991), in which he fabulates the Sino-Sufi order of the Jahriyya’s history of martyrdom as a pedagogy of the oppressed, a pessimistic means of being and becoming human capable of resisting the cultural disavowal of revolution in post-Mao China.
REGISTER (https://www.eventbrite.com/e/graduate-student-colloquium-with-connie-kang-tickets-1985605195591?aff=NextWeeksEvents)

UNDERGRADUATE & GRADUATE STUDENT PROGRAMMING

Information Session: Student Research Opportunity at COP31

Tuesday, April 7 | 2:30–4:00 p.m.

720 University Place, Second Floor

Are you a Northwestern undergraduate or graduate student interested in climate change or global environmental governance? Would you like to attend the Conference of the Parties (COP) (https://unfccc.int/cop30) to the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC)? Join us for an information session on the Roberta Buffett Institute's student research opportunity (https://buffett.northwestern.edu/programs/student-research-opportunity-at-cop/) at the United Nations' next annual climate change conference, COP31.

Accepted students take a fall-quarter POLI_SCI 390 course, taught by Professor Iza Ding (https://polisci.northwestern.edu/people/core-faculty/iza-ding.html) , that provides foundational knowledge of global climate governance and offers an immersive experience at COP, taking place in Antalya, Türkiye in 2026 (November 9–20). Students will gain firsthand insights into the negotiation processes and dynamics shaping global climate policy. Students will also complete an original research project, either individually or collaboratively.
REGISTER (https://www.eventbrite.com/e/information-session-student-research-opportunity-at-cop31-tickets-1982989183027?aff=NextWeeksEvents)

SPRING BUFFETT SYMPOSIUM

Neo-Hakawati Nights with Omar Offendum & Zafer Tawil

Thursday, April 9 | 7:30–8:30 p.m.

Josephine Louis Theater

Join us for a public concert to kick off the Roberta Buffett Institute’s spring symposium, Brave New Futures (https://buffett.northwestern.edu/events/brave-new-futures/spring-symposium.html) : a performance by award-winning Syrian-American rapper and poet Omar Offendum (https://www.instagram.com/offendum/) alongside acclaimed Palestinian multi-instrumentalist Zafer Tawil (https://www.instagram.com/zafertawil/?hl=en) . This performance is co-sponsored by A&O Productions (https://www.ao-northwestern.com/) and the Middle Eastern & North African Student Association (https://mena-languages.northwestern.edu/news-and-events/news/2022/mena-student-association.html) .

The show, Neo-Hakawati Nights (https://theaggie.org/2025/05/09/omar-offendum-bridges-hip-hop-and-arabic-poetry-in-neo-hakawati-nights-for-arab-american-heritage-month/) , is a spin-off of Offendum's off-Broadway hit Little Syria (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SkJFAueUrnU&list=PLbctIriiib9IS5JNCpx68afrTfUXl-vYA&index=1) . It's a genre-defying experience that blends spoken word, rap, electronically produced beats, and live music into a contemporary reimagining of the traditional Arabic hakawati practice. Hakawati is an Arabic word that signifies a communal storyteller who narrates culturally resonant tales, folklore, legends, and oral histories. Drawing from Offendum’s deep engagement with Arabic poetry and hip-hop lyricism, his performance style is an homage to the hakawati traditions of Damascus while remaining firmly rooted in the stories and streets of New York.
REGISTER (https://www.eventbrite.com/e/neo-hakawati-nights-with-omar-offendum-zafer-tawil-tickets-1982793448580?aff=NextWeeksEvents)

SPRING BUFFETT SYMPOSIUM

Brave New Futures

Friday, April 10

8:30 a.m.–6:00 p.m.

In person in Evanston

Online via Zoom

The second day of our public spring symposium will convene a visionary set of emerging international thinkers to explore how human relationships, information ecosystems, labor, and the planet itself are being reshaped in this moment of uncertainty and possibility. From the power of art for action to the future of work and planetary survival, this symposium will consider bold new visions for building more just and sustainable global futures.

subject:
Upcoming Talk of Interest on April 27th - Northwestern Center for
 Interdisciplinary Exploration & Research in Astrophysics (CIERA): Statistics
 and Data Science Undergraduate Programs
body:
Northwestern's CIERA Center invites Statistics & Data Science students to attend a networking lunch on April 27th, during which they will host Francesca Valsecchi, a Data Scientist who has worked across the across healthcare, finance, manufacturing, and sustainability industries. The event will be held from 11:45 AM - 1 PM, and lunch will be provided for registered attendees.

Students can [register at this link] (https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSep1tyqHXfZNHnoVe9f0Tr0R9pqkYJuR_8PJKN037K2DzDbhA/viewform). Registration closes on Wednesday, April 22nd.

The speaker's abstract and more information can be found below:

 

Abstract:  I spent 3 years on the academic job market before getting hard-to-refuse postdoc offers. During that time, I discovered data science, a career where I could use my research skills to tackle diverse problems with immediate impact. In this talk, I'll share my path from academia to data science in consulting, design, and climate tech, where I discovered that startups are where I thrive. Startup work lets me experience the full scope of my craft, moving beyond data wrangling to help teams deliver the right information to users, when they need it, in language they understand. I'll walk through my typical day and dive into Lineup, a venture design project where we built a restaurant forecasting tool through research, prototyping, and launching, as well as a COVID plot twist. You'll hear how I prepared for the transition, what academia equipped me for, and what I love about startups: the scrappiness, the fast pace, the collaboration, and teams that value both excellent work and creative play. I'll close with practical takeaways for your own career exploration.

[Flyer] (https://canvas.northwestern.edu/courses/22223/files/24430738/preview)


https://canvas.northwestern.edu/courses/22223/announcements/1806644






________________________________________

https://canvas.northwestern.edu/profile/communication

subject: You're Invited → EDGS Book Talk: "Communication Against Capital" with Rianne Subijanto
body:
** BUFFETT BOOK TALK
------------------------------------------------------------


** Selective Solidarity
------------------------------------------------------------

FEATURING

Inaya Rakhmani and Diatyka Widya Permata Yasih

Postdoctoral ethnographer at Leiden University in the Netherlands and author of Selective Solidarity: Children and Middle-Class Moralities in Transnational Senegal (https://www.pennpress.org/9781512827569/selective-solidarity/)

Wednesday, January 14 from 12:30 to 1:30 p.m.

Buffett Reading Room, 720 University Place, Second Floor

Lunch begins at 12:15 p.m.
REGISTER TO ATTEND IN PERSON (https://www.eventbrite.com/e/edgs-talk-inaya-rakhmani-and-diatyka-yasih-tickets-1978215699409?utm-campaign=social&utm-content=attendeeshare&utm-medium=discovery&utm-term=listing&utm-source=cp&aff=ebdsshcopyurl)

This talk unpacks the conditions of knowledge production in and from contemporary universities. Reflecting on the experience of producing knowledge from within Asia, it foregrounds Indonesia and Area Studies not as empirical site or disciplinary formation, but as a conjuncture through which to trace how inherited epistemologies, Cold War frameworks, and contemporary pressures of academic capitalism converge and generate contradictions that are historically formed and actively contested.

Join the Roberta Buffett Institute for a discussion on Asian scholarship with Inaya Rakhmani (https://arc.ui.ac.id/tim/dr-inaya-rakhmani/), Director of Academics of the Asia Research Centre at the University of Indonesia (ARC-UI) (https://arc.ui.ac.id/), and Diatyka Widya Permata Yasih (https://arc.ui.ac.id/id/tim/diatyka-widya-permata-yasih/), Deputy Director of Academics at ARC-UI. Lunch will be served beginning at 12:15 p.m.

This event is co-sponsored by the Equality Development and Globalization Studies Program (https://www.edgs.northwestern.edu/).

ABOUT THE SPEAKERS

Inaya Rakhmani is a visiting scholar in residence hosted by Laura Hein, Director of the Arryman Scholars Program (https://www.edgs.northwestern.edu/arryman-program/) within the Roberta Buffett Institute’s Equality Development & Globalization Studies Program (https://www.edgs.northwestern.edu/) (EDGS). Rakhmani is the Director of Academics of the Asia Research Centre at the University of Indonesia (ARC-UI). She uses cultural political economy to study media and communications as well as knowledge and information to explain broader capitalist changes. She researches the role of social and mass media in hindering democratic developments in Indonesia, with comparisons to India, Egypt, and Turkey from 2015 to the present day.

Diatyka Widya Permata Yasih is a visiting scholar in residence, hosted by Laura Hein, Director of the Arryman Scholars Program within the Roberta Buffett Institute’s Equality Development & Globalization Studies Program (EDGS). Widya Permata Yasih is the Deputy Director of Academics of the Asia Research Centre at the University of Indonesia (ARC-UI). Her doctoral research investigates the expansion of precarious work arrangements tied to the gig economy and its effects on workers’ subjective experience, identity formation, and organizing propensity in Indonesia. She is also a faculty member at the Department of Sociology, Faculty of Social and Political Sciences, Universitas Indonesia (https://scholar.ui.ac.id/en/organisations/department-of-sociology).

REGISTER TO ATTEND IN PERSON (https://www.eventbrite.com/e/edgs-talk-inaya-rakhmani-and-diatyka-yasih-tickets-1978215699409?utm-campaign=social&utm-content=attendeeshare&utm-medium=discovery&utm-term=listing&utm-source=cp&aff=ebdsshcopyurl)

Contact: buffettinstitute@northwestern.edu (mailto:buffettinstitute@northwestern.edu?subject=&body=)

https://buffett.northwestern.edu/

View this email in your browser (https://mailchi.mp/northwestern/communication-against-capital-03272026?e=5d73105ed6)
You are receiving this email because you subscribed to learn about upcoming news and events through the Roberta Buffett Institute.

720 University Place
Evanston, IL 60208
USA

subject: You are now subscribed to the DANCE-MARATHON list
body:
Your are now subscribed to the DANCE-MARATHON list (DANCE-MARATHON).

Please save this message for future  reference, especially if this is the
first time you are subscribing to an electronic mailing list. If you ever
need to  leave the list,  then you  will find the  necessary instructions
below. Perhaps  more importantly, saving a  copy of this message  (and of
all future  subscription notices from  other mailing lists) in  a special
mail folder will give you instant access  to the list of mailing lists to
which you are subscribed.  This may prove useful the next  time you go on
vacation and need to leave the lists temporarily to avoid filling up your
mailbox while  you are away.  You should  also save the  welcome messages
from the  list owners  that you  may receive after  subscribing to  a new
list.

To send a message to all of  the people currently subscribed to the list,
just send  mail to DANCE-MARATHON@LISTSERV.IT.NORTHWESTERN.EDU,  which is
called the list address.  You must never try to send  any command to that
address,  as  it  would  be  distributed  to  all  the  people  who  have
subscribed. All commands must be sent to
LISTSERV@LISTSERV.IT.NORTHWESTERN.EDU, which is  the LISTSERV address. It
is important to understand the difference between the two.

You may leave the list at  any time by sending a "SIGNOFF DANCE-MARATHON"
command  to  LISTSERV@LISTSERV.IT.NORTHWESTERN.EDU.  You  can  also  tell
LISTSERV how you want  it to confirm the receipt of  messages you send to
the list.  To send  yourself a  copy of  your own  messages, send  a "SET
DANCE-MARATHON REPRO" command. Alternatively, to have LISTSERV send you a
short  acknowledgement  instead  of  the  entire  message,  send  a  "SET
DANCE-MARATHON  ACK   NOREPRO"  command.   Finally,  you  can   turn  off
acknowledgements completely  with the "SET DANCE-MARATHON  NOACK NOREPRO"
command.

Note: For  users with a  valid Northwestern NetID,  subscription settings
and preferences can also be set using the NU Listserv Web Interface at at
https://listserv.it.northwestern.edu.  For  more   information  on  using
commands or on using the Web Interface to set your subscription settings,
see the List Subscriber's Manual at
http://www.lsoft.com/resources/manuals.asp.  For  users without  a  valid
Northwestern NetID, a  list of available e-mail commands can  be found at
www.it.northwestern.edu/listserv/howto.html .

Following  instructions from  the list  owner, your  subscription options
have been set  to "NOACK REPRO" rather than the  usual LISTSERV defaults.
For  more   information  about   subscription  options,  send   a  "QUERY
DANCE-MARATHON" command to LISTSERV@LISTSERV.IT.NORTHWESTERN.EDU.

Contributions  sent to  this  list are  automatically  archived. You  can
access the list archives at
https://LISTSERV.IT.NORTHWESTERN.EDU/cgi-bin/wa.exe?A0=DANCE-MARATHON.
You may need  to log in or get  a password for the Web  interface to view
the archives.

This  list is  available  in digest  form.  If you  wish  to receive  the
digested  version  of the  postings,  then  issue a  "SET  DANCE-MARATHON
DIGEST" command.

More information on NU LISTSERV commands  can be found in the NU LISTSERV
reference  card, which  you can  retrieve  by sending  an "INFO  REFCARD"
command to LISTSERV@LISTSERV.IT.NORTHWESTERN.EDU.

subject: You are now subscribed to the ARCHERY list
body:
Your are now subscribed to the ARCHERY list (NU Archery Club).

Please save this message for future  reference, especially if this is the
first time you are subscribing to an electronic mailing list. If you ever
need to  leave the list,  then you  will find the  necessary instructions
below. Perhaps  more importantly, saving a  copy of this message  (and of
all future  subscription notices from  other mailing lists) in  a special
mail folder will give you instant access  to the list of mailing lists to
which you are subscribed.  This may prove useful the next  time you go on
vacation and need to leave the lists temporarily to avoid filling up your
mailbox while  you are away.  You should  also save the  welcome messages
from the  list owners  that you  may receive after  subscribing to  a new
list.

To send a message to all of  the people currently subscribed to the list,
just send  mail to ARCHERY@LISTSERV.IT.NORTHWESTERN.EDU, which  is called
the list address. You must never try to send any command to that address,
as it  would be distributed  to all the  people who have  subscribed. All
commands must be sent  to LISTSERV@LISTSERV.IT.NORTHWESTERN.EDU, which is
the  LISTSERV  address. It  is  important  to understand  the  difference
between the two.

You may leave the list at any time by sending a "SIGNOFF ARCHERY" command
to LISTSERV@LISTSERV.IT.NORTHWESTERN.EDU. You can  also tell LISTSERV how
you want it to  confirm the receipt of messages you send  to the list. To
send yourself  a copy of  your own messages,  send a "SET  ARCHERY REPRO"
command. Alternatively, to have LISTSERV send you a short acknowledgement
instead of the entire message, send  a "SET ARCHERY ACK NOREPRO" command.
Finally,  you can  turn  off acknowledgements  completely  with the  "SET
ARCHERY NOACK NOREPRO" command.

Note: For  users with a  valid Northwestern NetID,  subscription settings
and preferences can also be set using the NU Listserv Web Interface at at
https://listserv.it.northwestern.edu.  For  more   information  on  using
commands or on using the Web Interface to set your subscription settings,
see the List Subscriber's Manual at
http://www.lsoft.com/resources/manuals.asp.  For  users without  a  valid
Northwestern NetID, a  list of available e-mail commands can  be found at
www.it.northwestern.edu/listserv/howto.html .

Following  instructions from  the list  owner, your  subscription options
have been set  to "NOACK REPRO" rather than the  usual LISTSERV defaults.
For more information  about subscription options, send  a "QUERY ARCHERY"
command to LISTSERV@LISTSERV.IT.NORTHWESTERN.EDU.

Contributions  sent to  this  list are  automatically  archived. You  can
access the list archives at
https://LISTSERV.IT.NORTHWESTERN.EDU/cgi-bin/wa.exe?A0=ARCHERY.  You  may
need  to log  in or  get a  password for  the Web  interface to  view the
archives.

This  list is  available  in digest  form.  If you  wish  to receive  the
digested  version of  the postings,  then  issue a  "SET ARCHERY  DIGEST"
command.

Please note that  it is presently possible for other  people to determine
that  you are  signed up  to the  list through  the use  of the  "REVIEW"
command, which returns the email address and name of all the subscribers.
If  you do  not want  your name  to be  visible, then  just issue  a "SET
ARCHERY CONCEAL" command.

More information on NU LISTSERV commands  can be found in the NU LISTSERV
reference  card, which  you can  retrieve  by sending  an "INFO  REFCARD"
command to LISTSERV@LISTSERV.IT.NORTHWESTERN.EDU.

subject: You're Invited → EDGS Book Talk: "Bad Lieutenants" with Andrew Mertha
body:
** BUFFETT BOOK TALK
------------------------------------------------------------


** Selective Solidarity
------------------------------------------------------------

FEATURING

Inaya Rakhmani and Diatyka Widya Permata Yasih

Postdoctoral ethnographer at Leiden University in the Netherlands and author of Selective Solidarity: Children and Middle-Class Moralities in Transnational Senegal (https://www.pennpress.org/9781512827569/selective-solidarity/)

Wednesday, January 14 from 12:30 to 1:30 p.m.

Buffett Reading Room, 720 University Place, Second Floor

Lunch begins at 12:15 p.m.
REGISTER TO ATTEND IN PERSON (https://www.eventbrite.com/e/edgs-talk-inaya-rakhmani-and-diatyka-yasih-tickets-1978215699409?utm-campaign=social&utm-content=attendeeshare&utm-medium=discovery&utm-term=listing&utm-source=cp&aff=ebdsshcopyurl)

This talk unpacks the conditions of knowledge production in and from contemporary universities. Reflecting on the experience of producing knowledge from within Asia, it foregrounds Indonesia and Area Studies not as empirical site or disciplinary formation, but as a conjuncture through which to trace how inherited epistemologies, Cold War frameworks, and contemporary pressures of academic capitalism converge and generate contradictions that are historically formed and actively contested.

Join the Roberta Buffett Institute for a discussion on Asian scholarship with Inaya Rakhmani (https://arc.ui.ac.id/tim/dr-inaya-rakhmani/), Director of Academics of the Asia Research Centre at the University of Indonesia (ARC-UI) (https://arc.ui.ac.id/), and Diatyka Widya Permata Yasih (https://arc.ui.ac.id/id/tim/diatyka-widya-permata-yasih/), Deputy Director of Academics at ARC-UI. Lunch will be served beginning at 12:15 p.m.

This event is co-sponsored by the Equality Development and Globalization Studies Program (https://www.edgs.northwestern.edu/).

ABOUT THE SPEAKERS

Inaya Rakhmani is a visiting scholar in residence hosted by Laura Hein, Director of the Arryman Scholars Program (https://www.edgs.northwestern.edu/arryman-program/) within the Roberta Buffett Institute’s Equality Development & Globalization Studies Program (https://www.edgs.northwestern.edu/) (EDGS). Rakhmani is the Director of Academics of the Asia Research Centre at the University of Indonesia (ARC-UI). She uses cultural political economy to study media and communications as well as knowledge and information to explain broader capitalist changes. She researches the role of social and mass media in hindering democratic developments in Indonesia, with comparisons to India, Egypt, and Turkey from 2015 to the present day.

Diatyka Widya Permata Yasih is a visiting scholar in residence, hosted by Laura Hein, Director of the Arryman Scholars Program within the Roberta Buffett Institute’s Equality Development & Globalization Studies Program (EDGS). Widya Permata Yasih is the Deputy Director of Academics of the Asia Research Centre at the University of Indonesia (ARC-UI). Her doctoral research investigates the expansion of precarious work arrangements tied to the gig economy and its effects on workers’ subjective experience, identity formation, and organizing propensity in Indonesia. She is also a faculty member at the Department of Sociology, Faculty of Social and Political Sciences, Universitas Indonesia (https://scholar.ui.ac.id/en/organisations/department-of-sociology).

REGISTER TO ATTEND IN PERSON (https://www.eventbrite.com/e/edgs-talk-inaya-rakhmani-and-diatyka-yasih-tickets-1978215699409?utm-campaign=social&utm-content=attendeeshare&utm-medium=discovery&utm-term=listing&utm-source=cp&aff=ebdsshcopyurl)

Contact: buffettinstitute@northwestern.edu (mailto:buffettinstitute@northwestern.edu?subject=&body=)

https://buffett.northwestern.edu/

View this email in your browser (https://mailchi.mp/northwestern/bad-lieutenants-andrew-mertha-03242026?e=5d73105ed6)
You are receiving this email because you subscribed to learn about upcoming news and events through the Roberta Buffett Institute.

Our mailing address is:
Roberta Buffett Institute for Global Affairs
720 University Place
Evanston, IL 60208
USA

Want to change how you receive these emails?

You can update your preferences (https://northwestern.us1.list-manage.com/profile?u=72adc4a52b41faa16985eef9e&id=4f5e55f4ce&e=5d73105ed6&c=ad9d9076b7) or unsubscribe (https://northwestern.us1.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=72adc4a52b41faa16985eef9e&id=4f5e55f4ce&t=b&e=5d73105ed6&c=ad9d9076b7)

subject: [EVENT] Chicago Energy Conference 2026
body:
The UChicago Energy and Climate Club (ECC) and the Northwestern Energy and Sustainability Club (NESC) are excited to announce the inaugural undergraduate student-run Chicago Energy Conference<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/www.chicagoenergyconference.com/__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!Q5m7--glageDFWyR2kiurf0uBjGTQqo8iEVx4HyRgzRkdssAcM0ubX74JZw2oe8hz8XQFGwbEer4y-_SL-7wbO36xcvlxIp3vLsIqzs$>. Taking place on April 11, 2026, at Lurie Medical Research Center, this conference will bring together students, professionals, and industry leaders to explore the theme: "Resilience in a Changing World: Stability, Strength, and Innovation for a World in Flux."



What to Expect:

  *   Keynote Speakers - Featuring leaders such as Dr. Scott Tinker (Professor and Allday Endowed Chair Emeritus at the University of Texas at Austin, founder of Switch Energy Alliance)
  *   Panel Discussions - Covering crucial topics such as Resilient Policy, Energy Finance, Cybersecurity and Grid Resilience, The Just Transition, Nuclear Energy, R&D, AI, and more.
  *   Networking Opportunities - Connect with clean energy companies at our expo and engage with panelists from Constellation, the Chicago Job Council, The Nature Conservancy, Radiant Energy Group, and other leading energy organizations.


Tickets<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/www.eventbrite.com/e/2026-chicago-energy-conference-tickets-1982321455837?utm_source=ig&utm_medium=social&utm_content=link_in_bio__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!Q5m7--glageDFWyR2kiurf0uBjGTQqo8iEVx4HyRgzRkdssAcM0ubX74JZw2oe8hz8XQFGwbEer4y-_SL-7wbO36xcvlxIp3d7if7G0$> are FREE for Northwestern students, staff, and faculty-reserve yours now!


For questions or collaboration inquiries, feel free to reach out at contact@chicagoenergyconference.com<mailto:contact@chicagoenergyconference.com>. Please check out the website https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.chicagoenergyconference.com/__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!VDttmg3IB-ck88Ga6deOpdRH-uonIJoi995w2Wg1Py9tlKd74HAd8hszNmWJ7b1f1YPmJVXSLFbWgtaz7a4R8_RUuJiFDA$ <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/www.chicagoenergyconference.com/__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!Q5m7--glageDFWyR2kiurf0uBjGTQqo8iEVx4HyRgzRkdssAcM0ubX74JZw2oe8hz8XQFGwbEer4y-_SL-7wbO36xcvlxIp3vLsIqzs$> for more details.


Looking forward to seeing you at the Chicago Energy Conference 2026!

Best,

Northwestern Undergraduate Energy Club
UChicago Energy & Climate Club

subject: Fwd: Tomorrow: Connect with MIT Sloan Admissions Advisors  –  For Select Universities
body:
---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Terrell Williams <terrellw@mit.edu>
Date: Tue, Mar 10, 2026 at 8:03 AM
Subject: Tomorrow: Connect with MIT Sloan Admissions Advisors – For Select
Universities
To: <nuisbe@gmail.com>


Join to learn more and ask questions about the deferred MBA offering from
MIT Sloan.

Dear ISBE,

Tomorrow will be my virtual, small-group advising and information session
for students from Northwestern University.

* Join me to:*

   Early

subject: [EVENT] Harvard Business School | Information Session
body:
Harvard Business School Information Session
Thursday, March 12th 2026, 5:00 pm - 6:00 pm
Norris University Center, Rock Room (2-270)

Join us for an information session to learn how the Harvard Business School MBA can open doors to all kinds of careers, from tech and startups to nonprofits and beyond.
We'll talk about college student opportunities to engage with HBS now, like Peek into Business Careers, Peek into the MBA, and the Summer Venture in Management Program. You'll also hear about the HBS 2+2 Program, a deferred admission option for college and master's students that lets you work for 2-4 years before starting your MBA.  Come see how an HBS MBA could fit into your future



Meg Flanagan, M.Ed (She/Her)

Associate Director of Student Career Advising

Northwestern Career Advancement

Kapnick Center for Business Institutions

subject: [STUDENT ORG] Alpha Kappa Psi (Business Fraternity) | Spring 2026 Rush
body:
Hi all,


Alpha Kappa Psi (AKPsi) - Northwestern University's premier business fraternity - is excited to announce its schedule for Spring Rush 2026!


AKPsi is a co-ed business fraternity that aims to develop its members into strong business leaders. New members partake in an immersive education program, creating bonds with brothers and other members of their mentorship class. From there, we encourage brothers to pursue career paths they are interested in, supporting them in any way we can each step of the way. Our brothers work at prestigious firms like Goldman Sachs, Google, BCG, Bain, Morgan Stanley, Amazon, Netflix, and Jane Street. Our community is more than just a network; it's a tight-knit group you can rely on for support and companionship throughout your entire career.


As a disclaimer, applications are already open! If you're interested, please apply here<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/forms.gle/DBkRuiw3jxxWU3Y16__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!RZWJV1dx0eYVC80EYzy954Q2aF5xrl3l55mUX0ZiHJtqs6bowZ-MhTWVtM7i3ijMQ8yfhY9laMu042HmcqShQX4jbmWH1aleXEA$>.


Rush Schedule:

*  Pre Break Info Session: Monday, 3/9 from 7:30 - 8:30pm in Tech LR2 (RSVP here<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/forms.gle/zWf1zt1538h5UXSF9__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!RZWJV1dx0eYVC80EYzy954Q2aF5xrl3l55mUX0ZiHJtqs6bowZ-MhTWVtM7i3ijMQ8yfhY9laMu042HmcqShQX4jbmWHht_KS6c$>)


*  Women in AKPsi Info Session: Wednesday, 4/1 from 7:00 - 9:00pm in Tech LR3 (RSVP here<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/forms.gle/9rd3yNnab1FVWafQA__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!RZWJV1dx0eYVC80EYzy954Q2aF5xrl3l55mUX0ZiHJtqs6bowZ-MhTWVtM7i3ijMQ8yfhY9laMu042HmcqShQX4jbmWHNu2HKgw$>)

*  General Info Session: Thursday, 4/2 from 7:00 - 9:00pm in Tech LR2 (RSVP here<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/forms.gle/eC9gTbPy4AVPwsWv8__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!RZWJV1dx0eYVC80EYzy954Q2aF5xrl3l55mUX0ZiHJtqs6bowZ-MhTWVtM7i3ijMQ8yfhY9laMu042HmcqShQX4jbmWHIcv0A0c$>)

*  Applications Due: Sunday, 4/5 at 11:59pm (apply here<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/forms.gle/DBkRuiw3jxxWU3Y16__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!RZWJV1dx0eYVC80EYzy954Q2aF5xrl3l55mUX0ZiHJtqs6bowZ-MhTWVtM7i3ijMQ8yfhY9laMu042HmcqShQX4jbmWH1aleXEA$>)

*  Rush Week: Monday, 4/6 - Friday, 4/10


At these events, you will have the opportunity to meet dozens of brothers and learn more about their experiences with AKPsi and at Northwestern.


Our official rush week kicks off with Networking Night. If you have a time conflict, please reach out at least 12 hours in advance to us at rush.nu.akpsi@gmail.com<mailto:rush.nu.akpsi@gmail.com>. Feel free to also check out our website at https://urldefense.com/v3/__http://www.nuakpsi.com__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!VhfQH_u31SA3HABbpfM0O6y86yc_KREN4E4lP30YMIZlKtXlL1mi0lErjWFGWcMxBaTAdz8pSoRQzfzlCjzDLCoVQ2iIqw$ <https://urldefense.com/v3/__http:/www.nuakpsi.com/__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!W0pJeA0AN61LmTDPuJtO3nxAm8eRcnriP05hfkvoTKCkOXvHAXhpyXNY_CCO9bsPyaVsNxrAT62Rc2guARpMY__iVUy7Qw$> for more information.


Please reach out to rush.nu.akpsi@gmail.com<mailto:rush.nu.akpsi@gmail.com> if you have any questions - we hope to see you all there :)


Best,

AKPsi Rush Chairs

Oscar Baliga, Evelyn Kim, Lance Lesser

*1. 2026-2027 ELECTION RESULTS*

Thank you to everyone who came to our elections and submitted absentee
ballots! After a long couple days of tallying up votes, *we are incredibly
excited to announce our internal/external presidents and treasurer for
2026-27:*


**DRUM ROLL PLEASE POW POW POW POW BOOM BOOM BOOM BOOM**

*Internal President: Alicia Li*

*External President:* *Aria Shi*

*Treasurer: Ethan Li*

YAYYY YIPPEE huge congratulations to the Li/Shi (lychee? Lizhen shi?)
dynasty and so excited for all the amazing things they will accomplish next
year! everyone in this ~holy trinity~ is a truly amazing leader so make
sure to stick around for next year 😁 and thank you again to everyone who
voted #yaydemocracy!


*REMINDER*: if you are interested in running for CSA exec board (coming
spring quarter 2026!), information about the other open positions will come
out soon (including mine! this could be u next year yurrrr). start thinking
about what positions you would be interested in, and please don't be afraid
to reach out to those who hold the current positions if you have any
questions !!

OUT!!!!*

get SOOOO hype because this friday we are incredibly excited to have

as our performers!!!! I am genuinely so excited i’m gonna pee my pants you

Additionally, HUGE shoutout to our student performance groups who have been
working hard to put on an amazing show for this friday, from dancing and
singing to kicking wood (shoutout taekwondo) <3 COME WATCH US:

*NU Bhangra, Brown Sugar, Deeva, K-Dance, Taekwondo, Treblemakers, and
Typhoon*
everyone has been working their butts off for this event so pls buy ur
ticket it’s gonna be awesome!!!!!


*LAST BUT NOT LEAST: we need volunteers to fundraise / sell tickets at
NORRIS this thursday and friday from 10:30 AM - 1 PM. *We are doing a
classic argyle bun sale and we would love your help!!! (we need your help)
(please)

Sign up HERE
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://click.mailchimp.com/track/click/30010842/docs.google.com?__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!Wwug5I0aaDo7s770kuTtiNebqtzJPaeOgpoWZxLj_3p-z_UGem5ejfcOQ0Y_zClH0J93lM7NT2x4AKmXIq5ssRuee06jmcMjFilhyJk$ >
to help table<3 YIPPEE


*3. CELEBRASIA AFTERPARTY*

because why would the fun stop… ;) *not affiliated with CSA** buy tickets
here
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://click.mailchimp.com/track/click/30010842/speakeasygo.com?__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!Wwug5I0aaDo7s770kuTtiNebqtzJPaeOgpoWZxLj_3p-z_UGem5ejfcOQ0Y_zClH0J93lM7NT2x4AKmXIq5ssRuee06jmcMj7fmZ0J8$ >!*

*4. PRESIDENTIAL FAREWELL ADDRESS*

with the new election results out, our resident uncs lovely current
presidents jerry and natalie must say farewell :(


*As our time as presidents comes to an end, we just wanted to say that it’s
been a huge privilege to be leaders of a club that’s been such a large part
of both of our Northwestern experiences, as well as (we think) the single
biggest club on campus. From judging a cooking competition (sorry Kenny) to
welcoming so many new members at countless group dinners, our time was full
of fun moments, with still more to come (hint hint Celebrasia).*



*Still though, there were plenty of times where we didn’t know what we were
doing, and so we’d love to extend a huge shoutout to all of you, our
amazing members, and especially our awesome exec board! ^^ and thank you
guys for learning and adapting with us. We hope you had as much fun during
Wu-Han era as we did!!!! Don’t think we’ve ever felt older than right now
😹.*

*We’ve met so many wonderful people along the way, and are so excited for
the future of CSA (aka you🫵). Say hi if you see us even after our
retirement!!! *


wow guys who else is emo af rn :(( we love u jerry and natalie!!!

ok that’s it for now 😛 see u.... at some point....
this might be my last newsletter guys


baby jb sends u all love <3

body:
---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Evelyn Kim <evelynkim2027@u.northwestern.edu>

Subject: Alpha Kappa Psi Spring 2026 Rush

Hi Gauri,


I hope you are doing well. I was wondering if you could pass on this
message to the WiB listserv on behalf of AKPsi!


Best,

Evelyn


--


Hi all,

Alpha Kappa Psi (AKPsi) – Northwestern University’s premier business
fraternity – is excited to announce its schedule for Spring Rush 2026!

AKPsi is a co-ed business fraternity that aims to develop its members into
strong business leaders. New members partake in an immersive education
program, creating bonds with brothers and other members of their mentorship
class. From there, we encourage brothers to pursue career paths they are
interested in, supporting them in any way we can each step of the way. Our
brothers work at prestigious firms like Goldman Sachs, Google, BCG, Bain,
Morgan Stanley, Amazon, Netflix, and Jane Street. Our community is more
than just a network; it's a tight-knit group you can rely on for support
and companionship throughout your entire career.

As a disclaimer, applications are already open! If you’re interested,
please apply here <https://forms.gle/DBkRuiw3jxxWU3Y16>.

Rush Schedule:

   -

   Pre Break Info Session: Monday, 3/9 from 7:30 - 8:30pm in Tech LR2 (RSVP
   here <https://forms.gle/zWf1zt1538h5UXSF9>)



   -

   Women in AKPsi Info Session: Wednesday, 4/1 from 7:00 - 9:00pm in Tech
   LR3 (RSVP here <https://forms.gle/9rd3yNnab1FVWafQA>)

   -

   General Info Session: Thursday, 4/2 from 7:00 - 9:00pm in Tech LR2 (RSVP
   here <https://forms.gle/eC9gTbPy4AVPwsWv8>)

   -

   Applications Due: Sunday, 4/5 at 11:59pm (apply here
   <https://forms.gle/DBkRuiw3jxxWU3Y16>)

   -

   Rush Week: Monday, 4/6 - Friday, 4/10



At these events, you will have the opportunity to meet dozens of brothers
and learn more about their experiences with AKPsi and at Northwestern.

Our official rush week kicks off with Networking Night. If you have a time
conflict, please reach out at least 12 hours in advance to us at
rush.nu.akpsi@gmail.com. Feel free to also check out our website at
www.nuakpsi.com
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__http://www.nuakpsi.com/__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!W0pJeA0AN61LmTDPuJtO3nxAm8eRcnriP05hfkvoTKCkOXvHAXhpyXNY_CCO9bsPyaVsNxrAT62Rc2guARpMY__iVUy7Qw$>
for more information.

Please reach out to rush.nu.akpsi@gmail.com if you have any questions – we
hope to see you all there :)

Best,

AKPsi Rush Chairs

Oscar Baliga, Evelyn Kim, Lance Lesser



-- 
*Evelyn Kim*

B.A. in Political Science, Minor in Chinese & Business Institutions
Weinberg College of Arts and Sciences

subject: ⚡️WiC  Bits: 2/25/26
body:
<https://wic.northwestern.edu/ >


Announcements at a Glance
 We know you're busy, so here are the highlights:


1. WiC Exec Board Applications Open!
*denotes a WiC Event

*✨WiC Exec Board Applications Open NOW✨*












WiC exec applications for next year are now open! If you’ve been looking
for a way to lead, give back, and engage more significantly with Women in
Computing, this is your shot. Make sure to fill out the application by
Friday, March 6th at 11:59pm CST. We can’t wait to see your applications!



📝Explore open positions: HERE
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/document/d/1xFOMuKeO2GzWpjmJ6vpqDpHjIOiXBHsHzKnq75ad9H0/edit?tab=t.0*heading=h.5y0ubbt7wuwv__;Iw!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!U-axmD7HPtZXvZcWNSU0kpS2jyboprobmmKzqGRXO1jizypBSpaAh7029el1wql6L8Yesk7EqfWif4qhIyai9VPNp8JbGE7giIXJ_w$ >
!


📝Apply HERE <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://forms.gle/WTjv7uJnW1cNxPFT8__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!U-axmD7HPtZXvZcWNSU0kpS2jyboprobmmKzqGRXO1jizypBSpaAh7029el1wql6L8Yesk7EqfWif4qhIyai9VPNp8JbGE5_76J4ig$ >!


­
What’s Happening On Campus


*Northwestern Dance Marathon!*















This year’s Dance Marathon is going back to its roots and will take place
for 15 hours at Norris over April 3-4, 2026. Don’t miss the chance to take
part in this historic year for an iconic Northwestern tradition. You’ll
have the time of your life dancing and supporting Bear Necessities
Pediatric Cancer Foundation and the Evanston Community Foundation. No dance
experience needed!

Sign up for NUDM 2025 HERE <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.nudm.org/__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!U-axmD7HPtZXvZcWNSU0kpS2jyboprobmmKzqGRXO1jizypBSpaAh7029el1wql6L8Yesk7EqfWif4qhIyai9VPNp8JbGE46SrQ-fA$ >!


*Accenture Coffee Chats at Northwestern for Sophomores!*















Are you a current sophomore looking to learn more about consulting? Join
Accenture for 20 minute coffee chats to learn about Accenture & the world
of consulting! We will be hosting them at the Starbucks located in the
Norris Student Center on April 10th, from 8:30AM-12:30PM CDT.  Shortly
after registering you will receive a note from us asking to verify your
time slot. Spots are limited, so sign up on Handshake today!


Link to register HERE
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://northwestern.joinhandshake.com/stu/events/1902198__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!U-axmD7HPtZXvZcWNSU0kpS2jyboprobmmKzqGRXO1jizypBSpaAh7029el1wql6L8Yesk7EqfWif4qhIyai9VPNp8JbGE6MMaQe_w$ >.


­
Other Opportunities



*Jane Street FOCUS Program - Applications are Open!*












Jane Street is inviting applications for its FOCUS program, an immersive
opportunity for first-year undergraduate students who have experienced
barriers to accessing advanced STEM opportunities. The program will take
place May 19–22, 2026 at Jane Street’s New York City office, where
participants will explore careers in trading, software development, and
strategy through classes, discussions, and interactive activities. No prior
finance experience required.


🗓 Application Deadline: Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 11:59 PM EDT
📩 Apply here: LINK TO APPLY
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.janestreet.com/apply-focus-nyc/?utm_source=program_email&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=focus_nyc_program&utm_content=apply_button__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!U-axmD7HPtZXvZcWNSU0kpS2jyboprobmmKzqGRXO1jizypBSpaAh7029el1wql6L8Yesk7EqfWif4qhIyai9VPNp8JbGE5tadwbvw$ >


Applicants will be notified of their status by April 1. For questions,
contact nyc-programs@janestreet.com.



*IMC Launchpad 2026 – Applications Open!*











IMC is hosting its Launchpad Program, a two-day discovery experience in
Chicago designed for second-year students interested in careers in trading
and technology. Taking place May 13–15, 2026 at IMC’s Chicago office,
participants will gain hands-on experience through a trading simulation,
build foundational knowledge, and explore career paths at IMC.


Students can apply to either the Software Engineer or Quant Trading track
(applicants may only be considered for one). This program is open to
students graduating in Fall 2027 or Spring 2028.


📝 2026 Launchpad Software Engineer Program <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://grnh.se/b9urzz82teu__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!U-axmD7HPtZXvZcWNSU0kpS2jyboprobmmKzqGRXO1jizypBSpaAh7029el1wql6L8Yesk7EqfWif4qhIyai9VPNp8JbGE7L2u6Yxw$ >


📝 2026 Launchpad Quant Trading Program <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://grnh.se/84qe8u02teu__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!U-axmD7HPtZXvZcWNSU0kpS2jyboprobmmKzqGRXO1jizypBSpaAh7029el1wql6L8Yesk7EqfWif4qhIyai9VPNp8JbGE7xWSBsoA$ >


Interested in learning more? Attend the virtual event, “IMC Virtual Event:
Launch Your Career with IMC,” to hear from past participants and ask
questions. RSVP here
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://imc.recsolu.com/app/collect/event/vUIHHdOdChqeNW5xnCfuSA__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!U-axmD7HPtZXvZcWNSU0kpS2jyboprobmmKzqGRXO1jizypBSpaAh7029el1wql6L8Yesk7EqfWif4qhIyai9VPNp8JbGE7U_GwvLA$ >!


Akuna Capital Tech Opportunities










Akuna Capital is looking for interns and entry-level software engineers for
2026 start-dates! At Akuna Engineers work closely with Traders and
Researchers to design and build low-latency, high-performance systems that
directly impact the firm. Akuna has a strong emphasis on mentorship,
learning, and ownership, making it a great environment for students looking
to grow quickly and make a tangible impact early in their careers. For
general questions or future opportunities, students are welcome to reach
out to recruitment@akunacapital.com.


Software Engineer (Entry-Level) - C++, 2026 <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://grnh.se/dpu46g741us__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!U-axmD7HPtZXvZcWNSU0kpS2jyboprobmmKzqGRXO1jizypBSpaAh7029el1wql6L8Yesk7EqfWif4qhIyai9VPNp8JbGE6X_d5kHg$ >

Software Engineer Intern – C++, Summer 2026 <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://grnh.se/i118vl5y1us__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!U-axmD7HPtZXvZcWNSU0kpS2jyboprobmmKzqGRXO1jizypBSpaAh7029el1wql6L8Yesk7EqfWif4qhIyai9VPNp8JbGE5-vqnYPg$ >

Software Engineer Intern – C# .NET Desktop, Summer 2026
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://grnh.se/ntlhoxs91us__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!U-axmD7HPtZXvZcWNSU0kpS2jyboprobmmKzqGRXO1jizypBSpaAh7029el1wql6L8Yesk7EqfWif4qhIyai9VPNp8JbGE5FwOtWtQ$ >

Software Engineer Intern – Full Stack Web, Summer 2026
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://grnh.se/efen49zw1us__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!U-axmD7HPtZXvZcWNSU0kpS2jyboprobmmKzqGRXO1jizypBSpaAh7029el1wql6L8Yesk7EqfWif4qhIyai9VPNp8JbGE5rhwPSsg$ >

Software Engineer Intern – Python, Summer 2026 <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://grnh.se/4gqo8lht1us__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!U-axmD7HPtZXvZcWNSU0kpS2jyboprobmmKzqGRXO1jizypBSpaAh7029el1wql6L8Yesk7EqfWif4qhIyai9VPNp8JbGE4j_K5dbQ$ >

*Summer 2026 at Build for Good Labs!*









Interested in building with AI + technology to support meaningful social
impact? [image: 🚀]


Build For Good Labs is recruiting students for our 2026 Summer Student
Builder Program: a free hands-on, remote experience where you’ll design,
build, and ship a real product addressing a real-world challenge. You’ll
collaborate in small teams with industry mentors, explore problem spaces
thoughtfully, and use modern tools to turn ideas into working solutions.
This program is about learning by building, gaining real experience, and
contributing to work that matters.

No perfect resume required. Curiosity, commitment, and a willingness to
learn go a long way.

Learn more + apply HERE
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.buildforgoodlabs.org/2026-student-builder-program__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!U-axmD7HPtZXvZcWNSU0kpS2jyboprobmmKzqGRXO1jizypBSpaAh7029el1wql6L8Yesk7EqfWif4qhIyai9VPNp8JbGE6Jcve9mQ$ >!


*D. E. Shaw Group — Summer 2027 Internships*










The D. E. Shaw group is now accepting applications for Summer 2027
internships in New York, with roles across investing, trading, research,
and strategy & business development. Opportunities are open to students
from all academic backgrounds interested in technology, finance, and
problem-solving.


Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, and interviews will begin
soon. Notably, offers do not have time limits. Additional teams and
international offices (London, Hong Kong, Singapore) will open applications
later this year.


If you’re interested in learning more or applying, check the D. E. Shaw
group’s careers site HERE <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.deshaw.com/careers/internships__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!U-axmD7HPtZXvZcWNSU0kpS2jyboprobmmKzqGRXO1jizypBSpaAh7029el1wql6L8Yesk7EqfWif4qhIyai9VPNp8JbGE4TYrxKzQ$ >!

*Summer 2026 Tech Internship Opportunities*

Looking for a tech internship for Summer 2026? This crowdsourced GitHub
repo  <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://github.com/vanshb03/Summer2026-Internships__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!U-axmD7HPtZXvZcWNSU0kpS2jyboprobmmKzqGRXO1jizypBSpaAh7029el1wql6L8Yesk7EqfWif4qhIyai9VPNp8JbGE7UuznnlQ$ >compiles a
regularly updated list of internship opportunities in software engineering,
data science, and related fields. A great resource to find and track open
positions!


*Other Resources:*

*WiC Alumni Database*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__http://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1iM1dULj3JIWcUlT5N_j1tXoU1ijYXph1JeNslt5zsMw/edit*gid=0__;Iw!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!U-axmD7HPtZXvZcWNSU0kpS2jyboprobmmKzqGRXO1jizypBSpaAh7029el1wql6L8Yesk7EqfWif4qhIyai9VPNp8JbGE6Fc5zFHg$ >

Northwestern Alumni Database & Northwestern Network Mentorship Program
<http://mentor.northwestern.edu/ >

Join a course specific Slack Channel
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1uRLNdxU_LvaIbKDbSIJlz5GJK79Nuea-JsauzMlYwgw/edit*gid=0__;Iw!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!U-axmD7HPtZXvZcWNSU0kpS2jyboprobmmKzqGRXO1jizypBSpaAh7029el1wql6L8Yesk7EqfWif4qhIyai9VPNp8JbGE4zWj2Rig$ >

Give us feedback or ask us questions
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSfOJ2c3m6w3WgZSMTHcDRRwgvZgCRXbESsd1wXnzRhdLtHBTg/viewform__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!U-axmD7HPtZXvZcWNSU0kpS2jyboprobmmKzqGRXO1jizypBSpaAh7029el1wql6L8Yesk7EqfWif4qhIyai9VPNp8JbGE6JdNZP-g$ >



*Stay in touch with WiC!*
Be sure to follow our Instagram @wic.northwestern
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.instagram.com/wic.northwestern/__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!U-axmD7HPtZXvZcWNSU0kpS2jyboprobmmKzqGRXO1jizypBSpaAh7029el1wql6L8Yesk7EqfWif4qhIyai9VPNp8JbGE5ihQ_6TQ$ >and LinkedIn

subject: Lunch & Learn | Sharon Oberlander (Merrill Lynch Wealth Management)
body:
Lunch + Learn: Sharon Oberlander (Merrill Lynch Wealth Management)

Kapnick Center (2010 Sheridan Rd.)

Friday, March 6th from 12:00 PM to 1:00 PM (CT)



Join Business Institutions for lunch and a casual conversation with our guest speaker, Sharon Oberlander<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.linkedin.com/in/sharonoberlander__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!WtUK8zTVwv2pOssxUt1rndv7XYIgitOyNEK23STB0ygV8xzMvSsJeSKdsCoDeL6L2qNo_uXVS0C9WUWwAeAae6KgfceZzg$ >. Sharon is a Wealth Management Advisor for Merrill Lynch. Sharon is a nationally recognized Advisor and a member of the Barron's Hall of Fame on their Top Advisor lists for the last 20 years.



What does a day in the life of a Wealth Management Advisor look like? Why do I think this is one of the most personally rewarding careers in finance? What is the impact that you have on families, your community and aligning with your values?



Topics Covered

*       What is a Wealth Management Advisor
*       Open discussion / Q&A



Register: https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://LL-03062026.eventbrite.com__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!WtUK8zTVwv2pOssxUt1rndv7XYIgitOyNEK23STB0ygV8xzMvSsJeSKdsCoDeL6L2qNo_uXVS0C9WUWwAeAae6KKRvDwng$ <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://ll-03062026.eventbrite.com/__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!WtUK8zTVwv2pOssxUt1rndv7XYIgitOyNEK23STB0ygV8xzMvSsJeSKdsCoDeL6L2qNo_uXVS0C9WUWwAeAae6K-5ZgbXg$ >



Custom lunches will be served. After registering, keep an eye out for an email asking for your lunch order.



Check out the full Lunch & Learn schedule<https://planitpurple.northwestern.edu/calendar/kapnick-lunch >! Lunch & Learn events are open to all Northwestern University undergraduate students.



----------

Natalia Zochowski

subject: [POSTING] Edelman 2026 Summer Internships
body:
Edelman Summer Internship Program

Posting Locations: NY, DC, Chicago, Atlanta, Seattle, LA, SF
Internship Program dates: Monday, June 8 - Friday, August 14, 2026

PR isn't just about being seen. It's about shaping culture and sparking real conversations. This summer, Edelman is looking for bold, curious, and culturally fluent rising seniors to join our Summer 2026 Internship Program. You'll be part of teams that have turned unconventional ideas into award-winning moments - from the viral Progresso Soup Drops that dominated cold-and-flu season and Cannes Lions conversations, to powerful social impact work like The Unburied Casket, a campaign amplifying awareness around South Africa's femicide crisis. Across digital, integrated, and experiential work, we build ideas that don't just get attention. They create measurable impact and shift narratives.

This internship is designed to give you hands-on experience, not just paperwork. You'll support cross-functional teams crafting strategy, content, research, and execution across PR, social, digital, and earned channels for culturally relevant work that earns media, inspires audiences, and moves culture forward. To be considered, you must be a rising senior, having completed your junior year of undergraduate study by Summer 2026. If you're eager to learn how world-class communications is made, from insight to execution, this is where you start.

Interns are selected to support clients through various capabilities (Brand, Corporate, Health, Impact, Strategy, Digital, Technology, Creator Marketing, Financial Communications, and Project Management) for the length of the program. In addition to receiving direct mentoring within a capability area, interns will also have exposure to other areas of the business to enhance their professional and communication skills.

To land this gig, you need to have a passion for people and helping others solve problems. Our culture is what sets us apart from traditional ad agencies, and it's why so many stay with us awhile. At Edelman, our most valuable resource is you - our people - with a diversity of backgrounds, ideas, and smarts (with heart). We support one another, and encourage each other, and we're looking for individuals who want to contribute to our culture.

Responsibilities may include but are not limited to:
*                Drafting press releases, media lists, fact sheets, editorial content, and much more
*                Learning the basics of your clients' business - i.e. product/service offerings, media targets, and competitors
*                Monitoring client and competitor media coverage and drafting coverage recaps for your team
*                Conducting social media monitoring and reporting
*                Updating media lists and pitching media contacts, under the direction of a supervisor
*                Staying up to date on industry news and trends
*                Participating in team brainstorming sessions
*                Assisting with the preparation of press kits for distribution
*                Assisting with research projects and new business audits

Qualifications:
*                A passion for communications and PR
*                Proven organization skills and the ability to adapt to new assignments, deadlines, and conditions quickly
*                Strong verbal and written communication skills - whether presenting to colleagues, drafting emails to clients, or participating in brainstorming sessions
*                Strong research skills and a demonstrated ability to become a strong writer
*                Developed interpersonal skills and the ability to work effectively with a variety of people across multiple accounts
*                Basic understanding of social media platforms (Facebook, Twitter, LinkedIn, Pinterest, Instagram, YouTube)
*                Working knowledge of Microsoft Office Suite
*                History of strong leadership skills through work experience and/or extracurricular activities
*                Demonstrated creative thinking
*                Familiarity with the public relations, digital marketing, or communications discipline through past coursework, internships, or personal experiences
*                Familiarity with AI tools and platforms (e.g., ChatGPT, Gemini, Midjourney) for content creation, research, and media monitoring is a plus.
*                Demonstrated curiosity about the role of AI in communications-how emerging technology is shaping media, storytelling, and audience engagement.

Additional Information:
*                Interns are paid $22/ hour and are expected to work a full-time schedule (40 hours/ week)
*                The internship is a 10-week program beginning on Monday, June 8, 2026 to Friday, August 14, 2026
*                In order to be considered, you must be a rising undergraduate senior having completed your junior year by the summer of 2026
*                The internship program will be hybrid-3 days/week in office
*                Edelman does not provide any form of housing reimbursement
*                Only students who are authorized to work in the United States will be considered
*                Failure to submit a complete application will result in ineligibility




Please see a link to the application here<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/djeholdings.wd5.myworkdayjobs.com/edelman-careers-E200/job/New-York-New-York-USA/Edelman-US-Summer-2026-Internship-Program_JR101401__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!Xi5UIQH61e6uwZ7cgsrLtbwRk-L4dvzYpnCxMVVGgF8pXjZyhQ1UQ10beLS97JeEv8LS5InPdPaxXyYYVm2q_DA36Q2dc-0xi2gAbZDB$>.

subject: Apply to campusCATALYST by Friday | A Chance to Gain Real-World Consulting Experience!
body:
Dear Students,

Want to gain real-world experience and make a real difference in the nonprofit sector? The deadline to apply for CampusCatalyst for the Spring 2026 quarter is fast approaching! Here's everything you need to know:

What is CampusCatalyst?

  *   Overview: Campus Catalyst is a business institutions class that introduces students to the world of non-profit consulting through real-world, project-based learning.
  *   Class Name: BUS_INST 394: Lessons in Non-Profit Management (Business Institutions minor elective)
  *   Purpose: Work directly with nonprofits to craft strategies that enhance their operations and impact
  *   Format: In-class case studies & Real-world consulting with a nonprofit client

What You'll Gain

  *   Real Client Experience: Partner with a nonprofit on a real case, creating solutions that drive impact
  *   Skill-Building: Learn hands-on techniques in nonprofit management and consulting, regardless of your current level
  *   Impressive Deliverable: End the quarter with a project you'll be proud to showcase

Class Details

  *   When: Wednesdays, 2:00 PM - 4:50 PM
  *   Instructor: Katie Hytros

How to Apply

  *   Deadline: Saturday, February 14th @ 11:59 PM CT
  *   Application: Apply here<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://forms.gle/M6NLrJ2kytbAaceR8__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!Qn0QP4kAr_s5BUmt10673X8DUZjO8IkP1hRtEy3sVdfaxdA5I8rq5xCEsL3D-70IEcCuC3aT-hst8V11W7jqW_r5OCAETQ$ >

For more info, check out our website<https://campuscatalyst.northwestern.edu/ > or reach us at campusCATALYST@u.northwestern.edu<mailto:campusCATALYST@u.northwestern.edu>.

Don't miss this opportunity to make an impact and grow your skills!

Best,
The CampusCatalyst Team

subject: [POSTING] NCA Is Hiring a Marketing Intern!
body:
Northwester Career Advancement (NCA) is hiring a marketing intern!

Details:

  *   8-10 hours/week for the 2026 Spring Quarter with the possibility to extend into subsequent quarters according to student availability and NCA need

  *   Hourly pay: $15/hour
  *   Open to current sophomores, juniors, seniors or IMC Masters students who are enrolled full-time on the Northwestern Evanston campus
  *   Reports to Northwestern Career Advancement Assistant Director

Responsibilities
Social Media, Graphic Design, & Content Marketing

  *   Assist with the development and execution of marketing campaigns for major NCA events, programs and services as well as branding initiatives using a variety of advertising mediums, including social media, flyers, web and email marketing.
  *   Develop NCA promotional materials, including flyers, posters, presentations, and TV ads consistent with the NCA brand and Northwestern branding guidelines; design materials using Adobe Create Suite or Canva.
  *   Maintain NCA social media channel on LinkedIn and Instagram.
  *   Research and identify new creative ways for NCA to reach students on campus through targeted marketing and advertising efforts.
Support of NCA Office

  *   Provide support during peak recruiting seasons, assisting with opening or closing the Interview Center, staffing in-person employer information sessions and/or interviews, and front desk check-in, as needed, based on student's schedule.
  *   Other duties as assigned.
Schedule Expectations

  *   The intern will be expected to complete most work in person, on site at NCA between the hours of 9am-4:30pm, Mondays-Thursdays
  *   Some offsite, remote work shifts may be added as needed, after a preliminary training period
Qualifications

  *   Reliable communicator. Responsive to emails.
  *   Demonstrated ability to proactively take initiative when building relationships and working on projects.
  *   Experience and interest in marketing, advertising, journalism, public relations/communications, design, or related field
  *   Intermediate knowledge of Adobe Creative Suite and Microsoft Office
  *   Familiarity with design programs such as Canva
  *   Experience in social media marketing (Preferably Instagram and LinkedIn)
  *   Excellent written and verbal communication skills, as well as strong proofreading skills; ability to craft targeted messages clearly and concisely
  *   Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail (double- and even triple-checks work!)
  *   Works well independently and enjoys collaborating as part of a team
  *   Interest in interviewing currents students or alumni for social media profiles
  *   Thrives on working under deadlines and managing multiple projects simultaneously
Apply:

  *   Apply here: https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://northwestern.joinhandshake.com/jobs/10708838/share_preview__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!WGQYqXP9kYSE0QQdutsahtitZZ80ccOEUlgmT6PYOLLmvMpglaXSMJaIBLv-J2xZ5xqVgmR1ZxErw6twHV7i7Pyn87YQoA$ 

  *   Applications are due Feb. 20 at 11:59 PM  (app via Handshake)

  *   Submit:

     *   A resume
     *   a BRIEF cover letter (2-3 paragraphs) about why you are interested in working with NCA
     *   A graphic sample promoting an existing NCA service, event, or initiative


Meg Flanagan, M.Ed (She/Her)

Associate Director of Student Career Advising

Northwestern Career Advancement

Kapnick Center for Business Institutions

Schedule an appointment with me<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://app.joinhandshake.com/stu/appointments__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!WGQYqXP9kYSE0QQdutsahtitZZ80ccOEUlgmT6PYOLLmvMpglaXSMJaIBLv-J2xZ5xqVgmR1ZxErw6twHV7i7Pz0RCoCZA$ >
