subject: ⚡️WiC  Bits: 2/18/26
body:
<https://wic.northwestern.edu/ >


Announcements at a Glance
 We know you're busy, so here are the highlights:


1. Buy tickets for SWEeheart Gala !!
*denotes a WiC Event

*3rd Annual SWEeheart Gala!!*











Mark your calendars! WiC is collaborating with SWE and more this year to
bring you the 3rd Annual SWEeheart Gala on Friday, February 20th. The gala
will be from 8PM to 11Pm at the Hairpin Arts Center (2810 N Milwaukee Ave).
All McCormick and Weinberg students are welcome at SWEehearts, with
priority given to McCormick students and participating org members. All
guests are encouraged to bring a +1 and they do not need to be a STEM major!

All tickets include a buffet dinner, desserts, non-alcoholic beverages,
live music, and a night to remember! Entry after 9PM is not permitted. *Buy
your tickets today for $8 per person **HERE*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScpIw0JNH1l9S2Gs5H8jKnxYA0iAOKZj_yphtTBh267Ur5xKg/viewform__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!Qnb7S4v23Abia7RdB9XaX3QliWEsXjNteuq-4uhqOiuCXXB7pMgw2PN76MuLoNA84JUwCaEcO7W3hU-VKJk_0Y_yc_Zb6hpbBzwvxQ$ >
*!*


­
What’s Happening On Campus


*Northwestern Dance Marathon!*















This year’s Dance Marathon is going back to its roots and will take place
for 15 hours at Norris over April 3-4, 2026. Don’t miss the chance to take
part in this historic year for an iconic Northwestern tradition. You’ll
have the time of your life dancing and supporting Bear Necessities
Pediatric Cancer Foundation and the Evanston Community Foundation. No dance
experience needed!

Sign up for NUDM 2025 HERE <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.nudm.org/__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!Qnb7S4v23Abia7RdB9XaX3QliWEsXjNteuq-4uhqOiuCXXB7pMgw2PN76MuLoNA84JUwCaEcO7W3hU-VKJk_0Y_yc_Zb6hrjVcw_PA$ >!


*SWE 50th Anniversary Celebration!*














Northwestern's SWE chapter's 50th anniversary is THIS YEAR! To celebrate,
we will be hosting a huge 50th anniversary celebration for both current
students and NU SWE alumni. Students and alumni will come together to
celebrate SWE's history, connect with one another, and participate in
professional development workshops. This two-day event is an incredible
opportunity to network with SWE alumni, so don't pass it up! Food will be
provided. You must RSVP
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScQKMnzG9G6NU3IUrjQaoaIo-TreASV5xm6-t8SFW-E-d5DXA/viewform__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!Qnb7S4v23Abia7RdB9XaX3QliWEsXjNteuq-4uhqOiuCXXB7pMgw2PN76MuLoNA84JUwCaEcO7W3hU-VKJk_0Y_yc_Zb6hrR1iS9ww$ >
to
attend.

WHEN: February 20th, 2-5pm - February 21st 9am-4pm
WHERE: Guild Lounge (Scott Hall)

(Part of this event also includes a student research poster fair, so if you
have research you would like to present, sign up for that separately HERE
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSfk8pAChYumJQ5-3gWx7sYYDzvuMrG4PfxYPiWtJ-99hNYADg/viewform__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!Qnb7S4v23Abia7RdB9XaX3QliWEsXjNteuq-4uhqOiuCXXB7pMgw2PN76MuLoNA84JUwCaEcO7W3hU-VKJk_0Y_yc_Zb6hrO3C56xQ$ >
).


­
Other Opportunities


NU Science Olympiad Invitational Volunteering on 2/21











Northwestern University Science Olympiad is hosting an invitational
tournament on Saturday, February 21, welcoming 500 students from 40 high
school teams to compete in 23 events. To make the day a success, they’re
looking for volunteers from 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM (you’re welcome to sign up
for just part of the day!). Volunteers will receive free breakfast and
lunch.


Interested members can fill out THIS FORM
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeu8Gcj9YMLFJgYudCEwGJPL183ixNheJeehCNn5nHeLchREQ/viewform__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!Qnb7S4v23Abia7RdB9XaX3QliWEsXjNteuq-4uhqOiuCXXB7pMgw2PN76MuLoNA84JUwCaEcO7W3hU-VKJk_0Y_yc_Zb6hq1c8tt6w$ >
and
indicate you are volunteering on behalf of WiC.


Akuna Capital Tech Opportunities for WIC Members










Akuna Capital is looking for interns and entry-level software engineers for
2026 start-dates! At Akuna Engineers work closely with Traders and
Researchers to design and build low-latency, high-performance systems that
directly impact the firm. Akuna has a strong emphasis on mentorship,
learning, and ownership, making it a great environment for students looking
to grow quickly and make a tangible impact early in their careers. For
general questions or future opportunities, students are welcome to reach
out to recruitment@akunacapital.com.

Software Engineer Intern – Python, Summer 2026 <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://grnh.se/4gqo8lht1us__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!Qnb7S4v23Abia7RdB9XaX3QliWEsXjNteuq-4uhqOiuCXXB7pMgw2PN76MuLoNA84JUwCaEcO7W3hU-VKJk_0Y_yc_Zb6hqW8fkUXQ$ >

*Summer 2026 at Build for Good Labs!*









Interested in building with AI + technology to support meaningful social
impact? [image: 🚀]


Build For Good Labs is recruiting students for our 2026 Summer Student
Builder Program: a free hands-on, remote experience where you’ll design,
build, and ship a real product addressing a real-world challenge. You’ll
collaborate in small teams with industry mentors, explore problem spaces
thoughtfully, and use modern tools to turn ideas into working solutions.
This program is about learning by building, gaining real experience, and
contributing to work that matters.

No perfect resume required. Curiosity, commitment, and a willingness to
learn go a long way.

Learn more + apply HERE
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.buildforgoodlabs.org/2026-student-builder-program__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!Qnb7S4v23Abia7RdB9XaX3QliWEsXjNteuq-4uhqOiuCXXB7pMgw2PN76MuLoNA84JUwCaEcO7W3hU-VKJk_0Y_yc_Zb6hoXY_yZ9w$ >!


*D. E. Shaw Group — Summer 2027 Internships*










The D. E. Shaw group is now accepting applications for Summer 2027
internships in New York, with roles across investing, trading, research,
and strategy & business development. Opportunities are open to students
from all academic backgrounds interested in technology, finance, and
problem-solving.


Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, and interviews will begin
soon. Notably, offers do not have time limits. Additional teams and
international offices (London, Hong Kong, Singapore) will open applications
later this year.


If you’re interested in learning more or applying, check the D. E. Shaw
group’s careers site HERE <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.deshaw.com/careers/internships__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!Qnb7S4v23Abia7RdB9XaX3QliWEsXjNteuq-4uhqOiuCXXB7pMgw2PN76MuLoNA84JUwCaEcO7W3hU-VKJk_0Y_yc_Zb6hoEkpUGiQ$ >!




*Trading Desk Operations Engineer (TDOE) 2026 Summer Internship*









Jane Street is currently looking for interns for Trading Desk Operations
Engineering roles! TDOEs
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.janestreet.com/join-jane-street/tdoe/__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!Qnb7S4v23Abia7RdB9XaX3QliWEsXjNteuq-4uhqOiuCXXB7pMgw2PN76MuLoNA84JUwCaEcO7W3hU-VKJk_0Y_yc_Zb6hquXEyRKg$ > optimize
infrastructure, support trading systems, and resolve issues on our trading
desks—everything from querying data to designing tools that enhance desk
workflows.

Interns work on real projects that impact trading desks, guided by TDOE
mentors. They gain hands-on knowledge of trading products and develop
technical skills through tailored boot camps. Jane Street is looking for
individuals who are  passionate about optimization and finding solutions to
complex problems.

Learn more and APPLY HERE
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.janestreet.com/join-jane-street/position/8075334002/__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!Qnb7S4v23Abia7RdB9XaX3QliWEsXjNteuq-4uhqOiuCXXB7pMgw2PN76MuLoNA84JUwCaEcO7W3hU-VKJk_0Y_yc_Zb6hq51UGenQ$ >!
*Applications
are evaluated on a rolling basis.*

*Summer 2026 Tech Internship Opportunities*

Looking for a tech internship for Summer 2026? This crowdsourced GitHub
repo  <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://github.com/vanshb03/Summer2026-Internships__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!Qnb7S4v23Abia7RdB9XaX3QliWEsXjNteuq-4uhqOiuCXXB7pMgw2PN76MuLoNA84JUwCaEcO7W3hU-VKJk_0Y_yc_Zb6hqf7MIQ_g$ >compiles a
regularly updated list of internship opportunities in software engineering,
data science, and related fields. A great resource to find and track open
positions!

subject: [POSTING] Chicago Bears Job Alert - 2026-27 Associate Development Program
body:
🏈 Chicago Bears Job Alert – 2026-27 Associate Development Program 🏈





 [cid:image002.jpg@01DC9B6E.6E4648C0]



The Associate Development Program is a paid 12-month full-time (temporary) experience designed for early-career individuals or those beginning their professional careers. The format allows for structured professional development and hands-on learning. A bachelor’s degree, relevant certification, or a combination of related education and experience aligned with the responsibilities of the role are required. Relevant coursework, training, or equivalent experience is valued.



2026-27 Associate Development Program Dates: Monday, June 1, 2026 – Friday, May 21, 2027



Learn more and apply at https://urldefense.com/v3/__http://www.chicagobears.com/jobs__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!TBdkGRslysaZOagTob4IkmV110UyO6o6YNhK00spQZ5ooRZiR7yMb7mDtonqEjM_ri2EgwUfGL6RNULVmaU3W7e7KAtGjQ$ <https://urldefense.com/v3/__http:/www.chicagobears.com/jobs__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!XdEY97bvMm6Akl-rpbnltYXsgB9YcTtfAo4znuzWEAqMRP1-cxd3bAnT2MnnzAAt5Mm3stHs3pYvZhRKNdYyOzwD9C4mMbeILQ$>.

subject: Harvard Business School Visit to Northwestern - 3/12
body:
Hi All,

Harvard Business School will be on campus on Thursday, March 12 from
5:00-6:00 PM in Norris (Rock Room #2-270) for an official HBS Information
Session.

The session will include an admissions presentation and Q&A, and will cover:
• The HBS MBA experience and student life
• The 2+2 Deferred Admissions Program
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.hbs.edu/mba/admissions/application-process/college-students-2-2__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!UkzEjIIMJa-m4JU6i3uj5LmVukIvrvme6G80lqh948ep4DUSJlVGMELWhOPXpRtxWPKgaXIFJh4QTxOLSKpdEA6ITaDu6PprtuAE4Ao58VGUsA$ >
• The SVMP application
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.hbs.edu/mba/admissions/college-students/svmp/__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!UkzEjIIMJa-m4JU6i3uj5LmVukIvrvme6G80lqh948ep4DUSJlVGMELWhOPXpRtxWPKgaXIFJh4QTxOLSKpdEA6ITaDu6PprtuAE4AoxWLOvOg$ > (both currently
open)
• Other college programs such as Peek

For members considering investment banking, asset management, private
equity, or long-term leadership roles, deferred MBA programs like 2+2 are
worth understanding early. Even if you are not applying this year,
attending can help you think more strategically about your path.

We strongly encourage upperclassmen especially to attend.

Best,
IMC Exec

subject: Pre-Registration for Spring 2026 | Business Institutions
body:
Spring Quarter Pre-Registration began Monday, February 16, depending on your assigned appointment time. If you are a declared Business Institutions student, you may register for a maximum of two courses during the Pre-Registration Period. Students may pre-register for up to two courses university-wide, regardless of how many majors or minors they have declared. A list of schools and departments that will participate in pre-registration is available online<https://www.registrar.northwestern.edu/registration-graduation/registration/pre-registration.html >. Please check CAESAR<https://www.northwestern.edu/ses/students/registration/tip-sheet.html > for your specific pre-registration time slot. Appointment times are set by the Registrar's Office a few days before pre-registration begins.

The Harvey Kapnick Center for Business Institutions offers pre-registration for courses with the "BUS_INST" prefix, as well as a few select courses from other departments (listed below).

  *   BUS_INST 301: Accounting
     *   Prerequisites: ECON 201 & ECON 202 or equivalents.

  *   BUS_INST 302: Marketing Management
     *   Prerequisites: ECON 201 & ECON 202 or equivalents.
  *   BUS_INST 303: Leadership in Organizations
     *   Prerequisites: ECON 201 & ECON 202 or equivalents.
  *   BUS_INST 304: Corporate Finance
     *   Prerequisites: ECON 201, ECON 202, STAT 210, MATH 218/220, & BUS_INST 301 or equivalents.
  *   BUS_INST 394-LK-20: Lessons in Non-Profit Management (campusCATALYST)
     *   *Applications<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/forms.gle/M6NLrJ2kytbAaceR8__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!WQaR9ArTV6XVlCahlPHTriarc5qwXwB4uz7oxey3c9m9ZkWTrPUCWGJ7GxCq2iM1Bp31h_2f5pgvVhtbIpdF9gK0uBqvEw$> were due by 11:59 PM on Saturday, February 14th.

The Writing Program will allow preregistration for Business Institutions students for the following class.

  *   ENGLISH 282: Writing and Speaking in Business

The Philosophy Department will allow preregistration for Business Institutions students for the following class. (Check CAESAR for FD/Distro/Theme Info.)

  *   PHIL 262: Ethical Problems and Public Issues

The Sociology Department will allow preregistration for Business Institutions students for the following class. (Check CAESAR for FD/Distro/Theme Info.)

  *   SOCIOL 302: Sociology of Organizations


When planning your schedule, please keep in mind the minor requirements<http://www.bip.northwestern.edu/undergraduate/minor-requirements/ >.

  *   Please keep in mind, you may NOT double-count Social Sciences & Humanities Electives with another major/minor/certificate. If applicable, Social Sciences & Humanities Electives could be used to fulfill Distribution<https://weinberg.northwestern.edu/undergraduate/degree/distribution-requirements/index.html >/ Foundational Disciplines<https://weinberg.northwestern.edu/undergraduate/degree/post-spring-2023-degree/foundational-disciplines/index.html >/ Theme<https://www.mccormick.northwestern.edu/academics/undergraduate/core-curriculum/social-science-humanities-theme-requirement.html > Requirements (Medill students must inquire with Medill for approval).
  *   If you are in doubt about a time or day for a class, refer to CAESAR or the department listing the class.
  *   There are no waitlists for BUS_INST courses. If a class closes, students are encouraged to check CAESAR periodically for an available seat.
  *   For important course dates (e.g., the final evaluation day), please refer to the "Class Notes" section in a class description on CAESAR.


If you have yet to declare the minor, you may do so after showing credit for the 4 prerequisite courses (Calculus, Statistics, Macroeconomics, and Microeconomics) on your degree progress report<https://www.northwestern.edu/ses/students/navigating-caesar/index.html >. Please refer to our website<https://businessinstitutions.northwestern.edu/undergraduate/minor-requirements/index.html > for more information regarding the minor requirements and declaration process. Students that have not yet declared the Business Institutions minor will have the opportunity to enroll in BUS_INST courses once regular registration opens (Monday, February 23rd, dependent on their appointment time).

Note: All major/minor declarations had to be received by Monday, February 9th to process them in time for the start of pre-registration. Declaration forms received after this date are not guaranteed to process in time for pre-registration.

----------
Natalia Zochowski
Program Assistant | Kapnick Center for Business Institutions | Northwestern University
2010 Sheridan Road, Evanston, IL 60208 | 847-491-2706
cbi.northwestern.edu<https://cbi.northwestern.edu/ > |Facebook<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/www.facebook.com/pages/Kapnick-Business-Institutions/253108394915__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!RIuSTPYKMq8F71u09NNMwtqGGKIfV2sY56xmuaqrND7HetqfsOar7BNFKErPBRe-hpWpRyaIwR1DwUIp1jD2VFnC3kxkrg$> | LinkedIn<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/www.linkedin.com/groups/4487429__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!RIuSTPYKMq8F71u09NNMwtqGGKIfV2sY56xmuaqrND7HetqfsOar7BNFKErPBRe-hpWpRyaIwR1DwUIp1jD2VFkcYaTfhA$> | Instagram<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/www.instagram.com/kapnickcenter/__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!RIuSTPYKMq8F71u09NNMwtqGGKIfV2sY56xmuaqrND7HetqfsOar7BNFKErPBRe-hpWpRyaIwR1DwUIp1jD2VFnUEs6KIA$> | Listserv<https://listserv.it.northwestern.edu/scripts/wa.exe?SUBED1=BIP >

subject: [EVENT] BHC Let ’s  Talk: Fireside Chat with Harry Kraemer
body:
We’re excited to invite you to our next BHC Let’s Talk event featuring Harry Kraemer, Clinical Professor at Northwestern Kellogg School of Management and former Chairman & CEO of Baxter International.

🗓 Wednesday, February 25
⏰ 5:00–6:00 PM
📍 Harris Hall L28
RSVP Here<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSemETnQ-M-P0chnc-rS4698gmooNPxyuRMws7CfekgkmIguIA/viewform__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!Xsh90AIN_Zo2w7P59pN83AiMl3DsfHzNPDyG2_BvaQ4qQH15brE_52TXeXvRgBl_vpBycUmVAnMNo9iNSc-KcB8751qwKA$ >

Join us for a fireside chat on values-based leadership, leading global organizations, and building a purpose-driven career. Professor Kraemer brings decades of executive experience and leadership insight. This is a rare opportunity to learn from someone who has led at the highest levels of business while staying grounded in strong personal values.

subject: [STUDENT ORG] Reel8 Productions (Video Production) | Exec Board Petitions
body:
Reel8 Productions, Northwestern's first unscripted video production club, is announcing our executive board petitions for the Spring 2026 - Winter 2027 cycle!


Who are we?

Reel8 is Northwestern's creative home for unscripted storytelling. We're building deeper campus connections through human-centric video content, inspired by the conversational vibes of Jubilee and The Cut. Our platform is a professional launchpad for undergraduate and graduate students of all backgrounds to get hands-on experience in production, digital marketing, and community building. Check out our previous projects on our YouTube<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.youtube.com/@reel8_productions__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!TT5ZOGPDwKj94atS7qrvEFJIyIoKcGbmN2tsiSuSy1NGFb8TBaQ6Kd2hLyd22NH7Ezpwe1hFUT7kSF5Eal6zKDG6B-OT5Q$ > and Instagram<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.instagram.com/reel8productions/__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!TT5ZOGPDwKj94atS7qrvEFJIyIoKcGbmN2tsiSuSy1NGFb8TBaQ6Kd2hLyd22NH7Ezpwe1hFUT7kSF5Eal6zKDHTcn_4HQ$ >!


Open roles:

  *   Director of Finance - Manage the organization's finances, including budget oversight, grant applications, and new fundraising initiatives.
  *   Director of Marketing - Spearhead our brand identity and digital growth by managing our social media calendar and content strategy.
  *   Director of Internal Engagement - Plan community events, manage internal newsletters, and lead bonding activities to keep club members connected.
  *   Technical Director - Lead hands-on workshops for video production equipment and serve as an advisor to the individual video producers and directors.
  *   Executive Producer (x2) - Drive the creative lifecycle of each video project by workshopping concepts, providing edit feedback, and mentoring individual directors and producers to keep projects on track.


More detailed role descriptions can be found here<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/document/d/1otcCZ5c8O9hL9ip3XeVlf0P4FG92eY8gAvznPz665C4/edit?tab=t.0*heading=h.u38qt8g4s886__;Iw!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!TT5ZOGPDwKj94atS7qrvEFJIyIoKcGbmN2tsiSuSy1NGFb8TBaQ6Kd2hLyd22NH7Ezpwe1hFUT7kSF5Eal6zKDH9FCkpdg$ >.


Important Dates:

To apply to an exec role, fill out this written petition<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSePaxFx_8HKIaGrcfLMafp8Onk7wti1WsyvyZZOQOWYIlm_Nw/viewform__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!TT5ZOGPDwKj94atS7qrvEFJIyIoKcGbmN2tsiSuSy1NGFb8TBaQ6Kd2hLyd22NH7Ezpwe1hFUT7kSF5Eal6zKDE9iS0P0w$ > by Wednesday, February 25th at 11:59PM. Also, sign up for a verbal petition here<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1MoQkNoMpSd3TsFFIae5skLxaPkVdJj0xRiJDmvRyNJQ/edit?gid=0*gid=0__;Iw!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!TT5ZOGPDwKj94atS7qrvEFJIyIoKcGbmN2tsiSuSy1NGFb8TBaQ6Kd2hLyd22NH7Ezpwe1hFUT7kSF5Eal6zKDGJ00KsEQ$ >. Even if you don't feel like you have all the experience listed, we'd still love to chat with you!


If you have any questions, please reach out to reel8prod@gmail.com<mailto:reel8prod@gmail.com>.


Best,

Reel8 Productions

subject: ⚡️WiC  Bits: 2/11/26
body:
<https://wic.northwestern.edu/ >


Announcements at a Glance
 We know you're busy, so here are the highlights:


1. Build Your 2026: Tech Pathways (Notion x WiC) *


2. Buy tickets for SWEeheart Gala
*denotes a WiC Event

*Build Your 2026: Tech Pathways*











Not sure which tech career path is right for you? Join *Notion × Women in
Computing for Tech Pathways*, a panel and open-networking event exploring
Software Engineering, Product Management, and Product Design ahead of the
2026 recruiting cycle.


Hear from women who have interned in SWE, PM, and Product Design as they
share honest insights on recruiting and day-to-day work, then connect
directly with panelists and peers through casual, role-based networking.


📅 Thursday, February 12 | 6:00–7:30 PM
📍 Ford Motor Company Engineering Design Center (ITW Room)
👥 Open to all students interested in tech


*👉 Registration required. **RSVP HERE!*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/inuvgia6?tk=R6gV5H__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!RxDW20YJL_5vYaEDw7S18SgtXT_E9Nf0xIdJsYHJ-ajo-cUDqzFBz-ZGPyu3QdFbICgFwu04r_44kUK2hOaptDPXLZmFMBezyJj75g$ >

*3rd Annual SWEeheart Gala!*











Mark your calendars! WiC is collaborating with SWE and more this year to
bring you the 3rd Annual SWEeheart Gala on Friday, February 20th. The gala
will be from 8PM to 11Pm at the Hairpin Arts Center (2810 N Milwaukee Ave).
All McCormick and Weinberg students are welcome at SWEehearts, with
priority given to McCormick students and participating org members. All
guests are encouraged to bring a +1 and they do not need to be a STEM major!

All tickets include a buffet dinner, desserts, non-alcoholic beverages,
live music, and a night to remember! Entry after 9PM is not permitted. *Buy
your tickets today for $8 per person **HERE*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScpIw0JNH1l9S2Gs5H8jKnxYA0iAOKZj_yphtTBh267Ur5xKg/viewform__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!RxDW20YJL_5vYaEDw7S18SgtXT_E9Nf0xIdJsYHJ-ajo-cUDqzFBz-ZGPyu3QdFbICgFwu04r_44kUK2hOaptDPXLZmFMBf7xuWYbQ$ >
*!*


­
What’s Happening On Campus


*DISC 2026 Family Applications Now Open!*













DISC is excited to announce that they’re bringing back DISC Families again
this year! This is meant to be a chill and fun way to get to know other
students in the Northwestern CS community in a small-group setting. To
participate, fill out a quick form and upload a slide introducing yourself,
and then DISC matches you with a "family", which consists of 1+ "family
heads" and a couple other DISC members. Families will then meet
semi-regularly to hang out and do fun activities of their choosing (in
addition to competing for glory at DISCOlympics, where families go
head-to-head at minigames). *Applications are due Sunday, February 15, 2026
at 11:59 PM.*


*Apply for the 2026 DISC Families Program **HERE*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdTUBOSmoZtkpYetsStWl2o7IhUXJmiH-t7zm6uYfi0E_5lLA/viewform__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!RxDW20YJL_5vYaEDw7S18SgtXT_E9Nf0xIdJsYHJ-ajo-cUDqzFBz-ZGPyu3QdFbICgFwu04r_44kUK2hOaptDPXLZmFMBeg3PuDpQ$ >
*!*


*Northwestern Dance Marathon!*















This year’s Dance Marathon is going back to its roots and will take place
for 15 hours at Norris over April 3-4, 2026. Don’t miss the chance to take
part in this historic year for an iconic Northwestern tradition. You’ll
have the time of your life dancing and supporting Bear Necessities
Pediatric Cancer Foundation and the Evanston Community Foundation. No dance
experience needed!

Sign up for NUDM 2025 HERE <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.nudm.org/__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!RxDW20YJL_5vYaEDw7S18SgtXT_E9Nf0xIdJsYHJ-ajo-cUDqzFBz-ZGPyu3QdFbICgFwu04r_44kUK2hOaptDPXLZmFMBe5eKZ8EA$ >!


*SWE 50th Anniversary Celebration!*














Northwestern's SWE chapter's 50th anniversary is THIS YEAR! To celebrate,
we will be hosting a huge 50th anniversary celebration for both current
students and NU SWE alumni. Students and alumni will come together to
celebrate SWE's history, connect with one another, and participate in
professional development workshops. This two-day event is an incredible
opportunity to network with SWE alumni, so don't pass it up! Food will be
provided. You must RSVP
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScQKMnzG9G6NU3IUrjQaoaIo-TreASV5xm6-t8SFW-E-d5DXA/viewform__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!RxDW20YJL_5vYaEDw7S18SgtXT_E9Nf0xIdJsYHJ-ajo-cUDqzFBz-ZGPyu3QdFbICgFwu04r_44kUK2hOaptDPXLZmFMBcbt60OGg$ >
to
attend.

WHEN: February 20th, 2-5pm - February 21st 9am-4pm
WHERE: Guild Lounge (Scott Hall)

(Part of this event also includes a student research poster fair, so if you
have research you would like to present, sign up for that separately HERE
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSfk8pAChYumJQ5-3gWx7sYYDzvuMrG4PfxYPiWtJ-99hNYADg/viewform__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!RxDW20YJL_5vYaEDw7S18SgtXT_E9Nf0xIdJsYHJ-ajo-cUDqzFBz-ZGPyu3QdFbICgFwu04r_44kUK2hOaptDPXLZmFMBdmMlKkKw$ >
).


­
Other Opportunities


NU Science Olympiad Invitational Volunteering on 2/21











Northwestern University Science Olympiad is hosting an invitational
tournament on Saturday, February 21, welcoming 500 students from 40 high
school teams to compete in 23 events. To make the day a success, they’re
looking for volunteers from 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM (you’re welcome to sign up
for just part of the day!). Volunteers will receive free breakfast and
lunch.


Interested members can fill out THIS FORM
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeu8Gcj9YMLFJgYudCEwGJPL183ixNheJeehCNn5nHeLchREQ/viewform__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!RxDW20YJL_5vYaEDw7S18SgtXT_E9Nf0xIdJsYHJ-ajo-cUDqzFBz-ZGPyu3QdFbICgFwu04r_44kUK2hOaptDPXLZmFMBegr0J0cQ$ >
and
indicate you are volunteering on behalf of WiC.


Akuna Capital Tech Opportunities for WIC Members










Akuna Capital is looking for interns and entry-level software engineers for
2026 start-dates! At Akuna Engineers work closely with Traders and
Researchers to design and build low-latency, high-performance systems that
directly impact the firm. Akuna has a strong emphasis on mentorship,
learning, and ownership, making it a great environment for students looking
to grow quickly and make a tangible impact early in their careers. For
general questions or future opportunities, students are welcome to reach
out to recruitment@akunacapital.com.


Software Engineer (Entry-Level) - C++, 2026 <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://grnh.se/dpu46g741us__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!RxDW20YJL_5vYaEDw7S18SgtXT_E9Nf0xIdJsYHJ-ajo-cUDqzFBz-ZGPyu3QdFbICgFwu04r_44kUK2hOaptDPXLZmFMBfJxLrLHQ$ >

Software Engineer Intern – C++, Summer 2026 <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://grnh.se/i118vl5y1us__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!RxDW20YJL_5vYaEDw7S18SgtXT_E9Nf0xIdJsYHJ-ajo-cUDqzFBz-ZGPyu3QdFbICgFwu04r_44kUK2hOaptDPXLZmFMBc3l-UAPw$ >

Software Engineer Intern – C# .NET Desktop, Summer 2026
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://grnh.se/ntlhoxs91us__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!RxDW20YJL_5vYaEDw7S18SgtXT_E9Nf0xIdJsYHJ-ajo-cUDqzFBz-ZGPyu3QdFbICgFwu04r_44kUK2hOaptDPXLZmFMBdPdwoWeQ$ >

Software Engineer Intern – Full Stack Web, Summer 2026
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://grnh.se/efen49zw1us__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!RxDW20YJL_5vYaEDw7S18SgtXT_E9Nf0xIdJsYHJ-ajo-cUDqzFBz-ZGPyu3QdFbICgFwu04r_44kUK2hOaptDPXLZmFMBczmMgaVA$ >

Software Engineer Intern – Python, Summer 2026 <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://grnh.se/4gqo8lht1us__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!RxDW20YJL_5vYaEDw7S18SgtXT_E9Nf0xIdJsYHJ-ajo-cUDqzFBz-ZGPyu3QdFbICgFwu04r_44kUK2hOaptDPXLZmFMBfYkSkmzw$ >

*Summer 2026 at Build for Good Labs!*









Interested in building with AI + technology to support meaningful social
impact? [image: 🚀]


Build For Good Labs is recruiting students for our 2026 Summer Student
Builder Program: a free hands-on, remote experience where you’ll design,
build, and ship a real product addressing a real-world challenge. You’ll
collaborate in small teams with industry mentors, explore problem spaces
thoughtfully, and use modern tools to turn ideas into working solutions.
This program is about learning by building, gaining real experience, and
contributing to work that matters.

No perfect resume required. Curiosity, commitment, and a willingness to
learn go a long way.

Learn more + apply HERE
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.buildforgoodlabs.org/2026-student-builder-program__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!RxDW20YJL_5vYaEDw7S18SgtXT_E9Nf0xIdJsYHJ-ajo-cUDqzFBz-ZGPyu3QdFbICgFwu04r_44kUK2hOaptDPXLZmFMBfil3fkAQ$ >!


*D. E. Shaw Group — Summer 2027 Internships*










The D. E. Shaw group is now accepting applications for Summer 2027
internships in New York, with roles across investing, trading, research,
and strategy & business development. Opportunities are open to students
from all academic backgrounds interested in technology, finance, and
problem-solving.


Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, and interviews will begin
soon. Notably, offers do not have time limits. Additional teams and
international offices (London, Hong Kong, Singapore) will open applications
later this year.


If you’re interested in learning more or applying, check the D. E. Shaw
group’s careers site HERE <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.deshaw.com/careers/internships__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!RxDW20YJL_5vYaEDw7S18SgtXT_E9Nf0xIdJsYHJ-ajo-cUDqzFBz-ZGPyu3QdFbICgFwu04r_44kUK2hOaptDPXLZmFMBfea2nZCQ$ >!




*Trading Desk Operations Engineer (TDOE) 2026 Summer Internship*









Jane Street is currently looking for interns for Trading Desk Operations
Engineering roles! TDOEs
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.janestreet.com/join-jane-street/tdoe/__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!RxDW20YJL_5vYaEDw7S18SgtXT_E9Nf0xIdJsYHJ-ajo-cUDqzFBz-ZGPyu3QdFbICgFwu04r_44kUK2hOaptDPXLZmFMBfmeqRgow$ > optimize
infrastructure, support trading systems, and resolve issues on our trading
desks—everything from querying data to designing tools that enhance desk
workflows.

Interns work on real projects that impact trading desks, guided by TDOE
mentors. They gain hands-on knowledge of trading products and develop
technical skills through tailored boot camps. Jane Street is looking for
individuals who are  passionate about optimization and finding solutions to
complex problems.

Learn more and APPLY HERE
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.janestreet.com/join-jane-street/position/8075334002/__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!RxDW20YJL_5vYaEDw7S18SgtXT_E9Nf0xIdJsYHJ-ajo-cUDqzFBz-ZGPyu3QdFbICgFwu04r_44kUK2hOaptDPXLZmFMBc6l4E9Og$ >!
*Applications
are evaluated on a rolling basis.*

*Summer 2026 Tech Internship Opportunities*

Looking for a tech internship for Summer 2026? This crowdsourced GitHub
repo  <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://github.com/vanshb03/Summer2026-Internships__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!RxDW20YJL_5vYaEDw7S18SgtXT_E9Nf0xIdJsYHJ-ajo-cUDqzFBz-ZGPyu3QdFbICgFwu04r_44kUK2hOaptDPXLZmFMBdzrslooA$ >compiles a
regularly updated list of internship opportunities in software engineering,
data science, and related fields. A great resource to find and track open
positions!


*Other Resources:*

*WiC Alumni Database*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__http://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1iM1dULj3JIWcUlT5N_j1tXoU1ijYXph1JeNslt5zsMw/edit*gid=0__;Iw!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!RxDW20YJL_5vYaEDw7S18SgtXT_E9Nf0xIdJsYHJ-ajo-cUDqzFBz-ZGPyu3QdFbICgFwu04r_44kUK2hOaptDPXLZmFMBdWAamQAA$ >

Northwestern Alumni Database & Northwestern Network Mentorship Program
<http://mentor.northwestern.edu/ >

Join a course specific Slack Channel
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1uRLNdxU_LvaIbKDbSIJlz5GJK79Nuea-JsauzMlYwgw/edit*gid=0__;Iw!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!RxDW20YJL_5vYaEDw7S18SgtXT_E9Nf0xIdJsYHJ-ajo-cUDqzFBz-ZGPyu3QdFbICgFwu04r_44kUK2hOaptDPXLZmFMBf79UtKQA$ >

Give us feedback or ask us questions

*Stay in touch with WiC!*
Be sure to follow our Instagram @wic.northwestern
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.instagram.com/wic.northwestern/__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!RxDW20YJL_5vYaEDw7S18SgtXT_E9Nf0xIdJsYHJ-ajo-cUDqzFBz-ZGPyu3QdFbICgFwu04r_44kUK2hOaptDPXLZmFMBfIZQT2NQ$ >and LinkedIn

subject: Apply to campusCATALYST by Friday | A Chance to Gain Real-World Consulting Experience!
body:
Dear Students,

Want to gain real-world experience and make a real difference in the nonprofit sector? The deadline to apply for CampusCatalyst for the Spring 2026 quarter is fast approaching! Here's everything you need to know:

What is CampusCatalyst?

  *   Overview: Campus Catalyst is a business institutions class that introduces students to the world of non-profit consulting through real-world, project-based learning.
  *   Class Name: BUS_INST 394: Lessons in Non-Profit Management (Business Institutions minor elective)
  *   Purpose: Work directly with nonprofits to craft strategies that enhance their operations and impact
  *   Format: In-class case studies & Real-world consulting with a nonprofit client

What You'll Gain

  *   Real Client Experience: Partner with a nonprofit on a real case, creating solutions that drive impact
  *   Skill-Building: Learn hands-on techniques in nonprofit management and consulting, regardless of your current level
  *   Impressive Deliverable: End the quarter with a project you'll be proud to showcase

Class Details

  *   When: Wednesdays, 2:00 PM - 4:50 PM
  *   Instructor: Katie Hytros

How to Apply

  *   Deadline: Friday, February 13th @ 11:59 PM CT
  *   Application: Apply here<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://forms.gle/M6NLrJ2kytbAaceR8__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!Qktm4eU99c0i8MEWu1v3nhT4VHmQD1fIr52JaLz1B7TZio-cXhR9D5CSzet5VV_-1e_AXCWJ4BKMK9Mzf1JmqNVB5J7Dh54$ >

For more info, check out our website<https://campuscatalyst.northwestern.edu/ > or reach us at campusCATALYST@u.northwestern.edu<mailto:campusCATALYST@u.northwestern.edu>.

Don't miss this opportunity to make an impact and grow your skills!

Best,
The CampusCatalyst Team

subject: SIGN UP!!! Lunar New Year Volunteering
body:
Hi everyone!

We’re still looking for volunteers to help run fun Lunar New Year workshops
for Evanston elementary students next week. The workshops will be brief
(~30 minutes), and they will include a brief presentation on the holiday’s
history, traditions, and fun facts, followed by a craft with the students.
It’s a great way to get involved with the local community! Transportation
there and back will be provided. If you’re interested, please fill out this

subject: Pre-Registration for Spring 2026 | Business Institutions
body:
Spring Quarter Pre-Registration begins Monday, February 16, depending on your assigned appointment time. If you are a declared Business Institutions student, you may register for a maximum of two courses during the Pre-Registration Period. Students may pre-register for up to two courses university-wide, regardless of how many majors or minors they have declared. A list of schools and departments that will participate in pre-registration is available online<https://www.registrar.northwestern.edu/registration-graduation/registration/pre-registration.html >. Please check CAESAR<https://www.northwestern.edu/ses/students/registration/tip-sheet.html > for your specific pre-registration time slot. Appointment times are set by the Registrar's Office a few days before pre-registration begins.

The Harvey Kapnick Center for Business Institutions offers pre-registration for courses with the "BUS_INST" prefix, as well as a few select courses from other departments (listed below).

  *   BUS_INST 301: Accounting
     *   Prerequisites: ECON 201 & ECON 202 or equivalents.

  *   BUS_INST 302: Marketing Management
     *   Prerequisites: ECON 201 & ECON 202 or equivalents.
  *   BUS_INST 303: Leadership in Organizations
     *   Prerequisites: ECON 201 & ECON 202 or equivalents.
  *   BUS_INST 304: Corporate Finance
     *   Prerequisites: ECON 201, ECON 202, STAT 210, MATH 218/220, & BUS_INST 301 or equivalents.
  *   BUS_INST 394-LK-20: Lessons in Non-Profit Management (campusCATALYST)
     *   *Applications<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://forms.gle/M6NLrJ2kytbAaceR8__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!Tj1R1s1FZAZab726gSou7uJ-94YoqCa92s142snAPm4QolQnKNZNDGwOxZ3-_Els5HwerMF5SZtNBLZXe3-m5FoCyvdFqQ$ > are due by 11:59 PM on Friday, February 13th.

The Writing Program will allow preregistration for Business Institutions students for the following class.

  *   ENGLISH 282: Writing and Speaking in Business

The Philosophy Department will allow preregistration for Business Institutions students for the following class. (Check CAESAR for FD/Distro/Theme Info.)

  *   PHIL 262: Ethical Problems and Public Issues

The Sociology Department will allow preregistration for Business Institutions students for the following class. (Check CAESAR for FD/Distro/Theme Info.)

  *   SOCIOL 302: Sociology of Organizations


When planning your schedule, please keep in mind the minor requirements<http://www.bip.northwestern.edu/undergraduate/minor-requirements/ >.

  *   Please keep in mind, you may NOT double-count Social Sciences & Humanities Electives with another major/minor/certificate. If applicable, Social Sciences & Humanities Electives could be used to fulfill Distribution<https://weinberg.northwestern.edu/undergraduate/degree/distribution-requirements/index.html >/ Foundational Disciplines<https://weinberg.northwestern.edu/undergraduate/degree/post-spring-2023-degree/foundational-disciplines/index.html >/ Theme<https://www.mccormick.northwestern.edu/academics/undergraduate/core-curriculum/social-science-humanities-theme-requirement.html > Requirements (Medill students must inquire with Medill for approval).
  *   If you are in doubt about a time or day for a class, refer to CAESAR or the department listing the class.
  *   There are no waitlists for BUS_INST courses. If a class closes, students are encouraged to check CAESAR periodically for an available seat.
  *   For important course dates (e.g., the final evaluation day), please refer to the "Class Notes" section in a class description on CAESAR.


If you have yet to declare the minor, you may do so after showing credit for the 4 prerequisite courses (Calculus, Statistics, Macroeconomics, and Microeconomics) on your degree progress report<https://www.northwestern.edu/ses/students/navigating-caesar/index.html >. Please refer to our website<https://businessinstitutions.northwestern.edu/undergraduate/minor-requirements/index.html > for more information regarding the minor requirements and declaration process. Students that have not yet declared the Business Institutions minor will have the opportunity to enroll in BUS_INST courses once regular registration opens (Monday, February 23rd, dependent on their appointment time).

Note: All major/minor declarations had to be received by Monday, February 9th to process them in time for the start of pre-registration. Declaration forms received after this date are not guaranteed to process in time for pre-registration.

----------
Natalia Zochowski
Program Assistant | Kapnick Center for Business Institutions | Northwestern University
2010 Sheridan Road, Evanston, IL 60208 | 847-491-2706
cbi.northwestern.edu<https://cbi.northwestern.edu/ > |Facebook<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/www.facebook.com/pages/Kapnick-Business-Institutions/253108394915__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!RIuSTPYKMq8F71u09NNMwtqGGKIfV2sY56xmuaqrND7HetqfsOar7BNFKErPBRe-hpWpRyaIwR1DwUIp1jD2VFnC3kxkrg$> | LinkedIn<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/www.linkedin.com/groups/4487429__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!RIuSTPYKMq8F71u09NNMwtqGGKIfV2sY56xmuaqrND7HetqfsOar7BNFKErPBRe-hpWpRyaIwR1DwUIp1jD2VFkcYaTfhA$> | Instagram<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/www.instagram.com/kapnickcenter/__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!RIuSTPYKMq8F71u09NNMwtqGGKIfV2sY56xmuaqrND7HetqfsOar7BNFKErPBRe-hpWpRyaIwR1DwUIp1jD2VFnUEs6KIA$> | Listserv<https://listserv.it.northwestern.edu/scripts/wa.exe?SUBED1=BIP >

subject: Fwd: ❗Important Announcement From ASG❗
body:
Sent from my iPhone   Begin forwarded message:      From:  Jasmine Ong <jasmineong2027@u.northwestern.edu>  Date:  February 6, 2026 at 3:20:44 PM CST  To:  Grace Shao <graceshao2026@u.northwestern.edu>  Subject:   Fwd: ❗Important Announcement From ASG❗       ﻿

  


  
 Warm wishes, 
  ​ Jasmine Ong   (she/her)  
​Northwestern University | Class of 2027 
​B.S. ​Industrial Engineering, Minors in Sociology and Science in Human Culture  
   LinkedIn  | (773) 242-5877  
 
 ---------- Forwarded message ---------- 
 From:  Student Organization Finance Office <sofo@northwestern.edu> 
 Date:  Feb 6, 2026 at 3:14 PM -0600 
 To:  SOFO-OFFICERS@listserv.it.northwestern.edu 
 Subject:  ❗Important Announcement From ASG❗ 
 
 

 
 
  
  
 
 
  
  
 
  
 
 
  
   
 
  
 
 
 
  
 
  
 
 
 
  
 
 
  
 
 
  
 
 
  
 
 
  
 
   Important Message from ASG 
 
 
  
 
 
 
 
   
 
 
 
 
 Good afternoon, Student Organization leaders, 
 
My name is John Sisco, one of the co-chairs of ASG’s Student Activities Finance Committee. This email is intended to notify ASG-affiliated presidents and treasurers of a new opportunity for organizations to reallocate how their ASG funds are used throughout the academic year. 
 
One frequent concern student organizations express is the fact that their funding needs may change throughout the year in a way that their 2025-2026 Spring Funding Allocation does not reflect. While it is important that ASG has a strong understanding of how student groups intend to use SAFC funding throughout the year, we know it is impossible for organizations to predict all of their potential financial needs nearly a year in advance. This can lead to difficulties with receiving approval for needed expenditures that do not reflect the previous year’s funding request. 
 
For that reason, the attached form will now allow student groups which received SAFC funding in the 2025-2026 Spring Funding Cycle to petition to reallocate their funds for purposes not originally outlined in their funding application. The Student Activities Finance Committee will review these applications on a weekly basis, and will inform student groups promptly in the event reallocation is approved. Please consult the attached form for additional details regarding funding guidelines, policies, and restrictions. Also note that this is not an additional source of ASG funding (please consult other opportunities like the ASG executive grant or Senate funding), but instead a means to reallocate previously granted funds. 
 
Thank you so much for your dedication to keeping student life on Northwestern’s campus dynamic and accessible! Please feel free to contact myself or my co-chairs Sydney-Reese Harris and Gauri Adarsh at ASG-finance@u.northwestern.edu for any additional questions, input, or concerns.  
 
 
 
 
 
  
   SAFC Funding Reallocation Form   
 
  
 
 
  
 
 
  
 
 
  
 
 
  
 
 
 
  
  
 
  
  
 
  
 
 
 
 
  
 
     Unsubscribe   
 
 Norris University Center 
1999 Campus Drive 
Evanston, IL 60208 
 
  (847) 491-2328   
  SOFO@northwestern.edu   
  https://www.northwestern.edu/norris/services/sofo/   
 
 Thank you!

subject: [EVENT] Career 101 Panel: Navigating Early Career Paths (Presented by UES)
body:
Career 101 Panel: Navigating Early Career Paths
Still trying to figure out how to land a summer internship or what people actually do with an econ major? Come to our Career 101 panel featuring Northwestern upperclassmen econ majors across wealth management, consulting, marketing, and more! They’ve been through recruiting  and are excited to share what worked, what didn’t, and what they wish they knew earlier.

Whether you are a first-year, sophomore, or just want to learn more about what you can do with your economics major, this panel is for you. Bring Friends and questions!

Please fill out this interest form<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSfDKd7aY8OrzICvS_xJmbn4SvYJ3g77OyKzQZ9jyHbdJeCRNw/viewform__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!TLkSLshuFD60uhWDLyf_dH12EiGs_BvO40DVuOkej9rW30KTajhC0wDNa8blK4xcKk86Y-MGjUCBWB3atQeYhNidFQKR7ypBvv33xcw$> if you plan to attend.

subject: Lunch & Learn | Financial Investment Management & Insurance
body:
Lunch + Learn: Deanna Strable-Soethout (Financial Investment Management & Insurance)

Kapnick Center (2010 Sheridan Rd.)

Thursday, February 12th from 12:00 PM to 1:00 PM (CT)



Join Business Institutions for lunch and a casual conversation with our guest speaker, Deanna Strable-Soethout<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.linkedin.com/in/deanna-strable-soethout-6a691229/__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!QKT3aOWpD0AeJ0E6DsZVbExf-509bS-71X7ijbfvXDJfohbGZ28NoYygZ7AShOLzJxdW8ajJ8AOtW5cA99HiVxFfc4LQOg$ > of Principal Financial Group<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.principal.com/__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!QKT3aOWpD0AeJ0E6DsZVbExf-509bS-71X7ijbfvXDJfohbGZ28NoYygZ7AShOLzJxdW8ajJ8AOtW5cA99HiVxFWh5jNMQ$ >.



Deanna Strable-Soethout is an American business executive and actuary who is the Chief executive officer (CEO) and Executive Chair of the Board at Principal Financial Group. Deanna Strable-Soethout became CEO of Principal Financial Group in January 2025. She joined the company in 1990 as an actuarial associate and held several positions over the course of her career at PFP, which spans over three decades. Most recently, she held roles of Senior Vice President, President of U.S. Insurance Solutions, and Chief Financial Officer. She was named President & Chief Operating Officer in 2024.



Principal Financial Group, Inc. (PFG) is an American global financial investment management and insurance company headquartered in Des Moines, Iowa, United States. PFG is dedicated to improving the wealth and well-being of people and businesses around the world-helping more than 62M customers plan, protect, invest, and retire.



Topics Covered

3.      Career path and overview of industry.
4.      Open discussion / Q&A



Register: https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://LL-02122026.eventbrite.com__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!QKT3aOWpD0AeJ0E6DsZVbExf-509bS-71X7ijbfvXDJfohbGZ28NoYygZ7AShOLzJxdW8ajJ8AOtW5cA99HiVxE8tZzr0A$ <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://ll-02122026.eventbrite.com/__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!QKT3aOWpD0AeJ0E6DsZVbExf-509bS-71X7ijbfvXDJfohbGZ28NoYygZ7AShOLzJxdW8ajJ8AOtW5cA99HiVxEkkgk2xA$ >



Custom lunches will be served. After registering, keep an eye out for an email asking for your lunch order.



Check out the full Lunch & Learn schedule<https://planitpurple.northwestern.edu/calendar/kapnick-lunch >! Lunch & Learn events are open to all Northwestern University undergraduate students.



----------

Natalia Zochowski
Program Assistant | Kapnick Center for Business Institutions | Northwestern University
2010 Sheridan Road, Evanston, IL 60208 | 847-491-2706
cbi.northwestern.edu<https://cbi.northwestern.edu/ > |Facebook<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.facebook.com/pages/Kapnick-Business-Institutions/253108394915__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!QKT3aOWpD0AeJ0E6DsZVbExf-509bS-71X7ijbfvXDJfohbGZ28NoYygZ7AShOLzJxdW8ajJ8AOtW5cA99HiVxHWmY83fA$ > | LinkedIn<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.linkedin.com/groups/4487429__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!QKT3aOWpD0AeJ0E6DsZVbExf-509bS-71X7ijbfvXDJfohbGZ28NoYygZ7AShOLzJxdW8ajJ8AOtW5cA99HiVxFTiZXqIQ$ > | Instagram<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.instagram.com/kapnickcenter/__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!QKT3aOWpD0AeJ0E6DsZVbExf-509bS-71X7ijbfvXDJfohbGZ28NoYygZ7AShOLzJxdW8ajJ8AOtW5cA99HiVxFkUZhZ-A$ > | Listserv<https://listserv.it.northwestern.edu/scripts/wa.exe?SUBED1=BIP >

subject: ⚡️WiC  Bits: 2/4/26
body:
<https://wic.northwestern.edu/ >


Announcements at a Glance
 We know you're busy, so here are the highlights:


1. Build Your 2026: Tech Pathways (Notion x WiC) *
*denotes a WiC Event

*Build Your 2026: Tech Pathways*











Not sure which tech career path is right for you? Join *Notion × Women in
Computing for Tech Pathways*, a panel and open-networking event exploring
Software Engineering, Product Management, and Product Design ahead of the
2026 recruiting cycle.


Hear from women who have interned in SWE, PM, and Product Design as they
share honest insights on recruiting and day-to-day work, then connect
directly with panelists and peers through casual, role-based networking.


📅 Thursday, February 12 | 6:00–7:30 PM
📍 Ford Motor Company Engineering Design Center (ITW Room)
👥 Open to all students interested in tech


👉 Registration required. RSVP HERE! <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/inuvgia6?tk=R6gV5H__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!WQiQ2jPYTiz-0cuwrCAyXyhJCdT-xBXzxmSs_RKwxX9yukvqI33qK7cTzNR-ovg58ukRX6Jw2djYGF0uDjPoS8641ZCdWawSFxqPmQ$ >

*3rd Annual SWEetheart Gala!*











Mark your calendars! WiC is collaborating with SWE and more this year to
bring you the 3rd Annual SWEetheart Gala on Friday, February 20th. The gala
will be from 8PM to 11Pm at the Hairpin Arts Center (2810 N Milwaukee Ave).
All McCormick and Weinberg students are welcome at SWEethearts, with
priority given to McCormick students and participating org members. All
guests are encouraged to bring a +1 and they do not need to be a STEM major!

Tickets will be available on Friday, February 6th, and are $8 per person.
All tickets include a buffet dinner, desserts, non-alcoholic beverages,
live music, and a night to remember! Entry after 9PM is not permitted. Keep
a lookout for the link to register!


­
What’s Happening On Campus


*DISC Family Head Application Now Open!*












DISC is excited to announce that they’re bringing back DISC Families again
this year! This is meant to be a chill and fun way to get to know other
students in the DISC community in a small-group setting. To apply,
fill out THIS
FORM
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSc0Yo_A1mvm7k1IUlpIw0AprhZRttXyKj-cuJq3tdHy2LmC1A/viewform__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!WQiQ2jPYTiz-0cuwrCAyXyhJCdT-xBXzxmSs_RKwxX9yukvqI33qK7cTzNR-ovg58ukRX6Jw2djYGF0uDjPoS8641ZCdWay7mKvRHg$ >
by
5PM next Monday (February 9th) and upload a slide introducing yourself, and
then we match you with a "family", which consists of 1+ "family heads" and
a couple other DISC members. Families will then meet semi-regularly to hang
out and do fun activities of their choosing (in addition to competing for
glory at DISCOlympics, where families go head-to-head at minigames).


In addition to DISC exec being family heads, anyone who is at least a
sophomore can apply to be a family head! Families can have more than 1
family head. If you'd like to be placed in a family as a non-family-head,
we'll release the form for that next week!


*Northwestern Dance Marathon!*















This year’s Dance Marathon is going back to its roots and will take place
for 15 hours at Norris over April 3-4, 2026. Don’t miss the chance to take
part in this historic year for an iconic Northwestern tradition. You’ll
have the time of your life dancing and supporting Bear Necessities
Pediatric Cancer Foundation and the Evanston Community Foundation. No dance
experience needed!

Sign up for NUDM 2025 HERE <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.nudm.org/__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!WQiQ2jPYTiz-0cuwrCAyXyhJCdT-xBXzxmSs_RKwxX9yukvqI33qK7cTzNR-ovg58ukRX6Jw2djYGF0uDjPoS8641ZCdWax0gh-Uug$ >!


*SWE 50th Anniversary Celebration!*














Northwestern's SWE chapter's 50th anniversary is THIS YEAR! To celebrate,
we will be hosting a huge 50th anniversary celebration for both current
students and NU SWE alumni. Students and alumni will come together to
celebrate SWE's history, connect with one another, and participate in
professional development workshops. This two-day event is an incredible
opportunity to network with SWE alumni, so don't pass it up! Food will be
provided. You must RSVP
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScQKMnzG9G6NU3IUrjQaoaIo-TreASV5xm6-t8SFW-E-d5DXA/viewform__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!WQiQ2jPYTiz-0cuwrCAyXyhJCdT-xBXzxmSs_RKwxX9yukvqI33qK7cTzNR-ovg58ukRX6Jw2djYGF0uDjPoS8641ZCdWaxDHk5IAA$ >
to
attend.

WHEN: February 20th, 2-5pm - February 21st 9am-4pm
WHERE: Guild Lounge (Scott Hall)

(Part of this event also includes a student research poster fair, so if you
have research you would like to present, sign up for that separately HERE
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSfk8pAChYumJQ5-3gWx7sYYDzvuMrG4PfxYPiWtJ-99hNYADg/viewform__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!WQiQ2jPYTiz-0cuwrCAyXyhJCdT-xBXzxmSs_RKwxX9yukvqI33qK7cTzNR-ovg58ukRX6Jw2djYGF0uDjPoS8641ZCdWawEUtOslg$ >
).


­
Other Opportunities


Akuna Capital Tech Opportunities for WIC Members










Akuna Capital is looking for interns and entry-level software engineers for
2026 start-dates! At Akuna Engineers work closely with Traders and
Researchers to design and build low-latency, high-performance systems that
directly impact the firm. Akuna has a strong emphasis on mentorship,
learning, and ownership, making it a great environment for students looking
to grow quickly and make a tangible impact early in their careers. For
general questions or future opportunities, students are welcome to reach
out to recruitment@akunacapital.com.


Software Engineer (Entry-Level) - C++, 2026 <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://grnh.se/dpu46g741us__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!WQiQ2jPYTiz-0cuwrCAyXyhJCdT-xBXzxmSs_RKwxX9yukvqI33qK7cTzNR-ovg58ukRX6Jw2djYGF0uDjPoS8641ZCdWazswF8DzQ$ >

Software Engineer Intern – C++, Summer 2026 <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://grnh.se/i118vl5y1us__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!WQiQ2jPYTiz-0cuwrCAyXyhJCdT-xBXzxmSs_RKwxX9yukvqI33qK7cTzNR-ovg58ukRX6Jw2djYGF0uDjPoS8641ZCdWaxWr_Pe-A$ >

Software Engineer Intern – C# .NET Desktop, Summer 2026
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://grnh.se/ntlhoxs91us__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!WQiQ2jPYTiz-0cuwrCAyXyhJCdT-xBXzxmSs_RKwxX9yukvqI33qK7cTzNR-ovg58ukRX6Jw2djYGF0uDjPoS8641ZCdWayoinIOLw$ >

Software Engineer Intern – Full Stack Web, Summer 2026
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://grnh.se/efen49zw1us__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!WQiQ2jPYTiz-0cuwrCAyXyhJCdT-xBXzxmSs_RKwxX9yukvqI33qK7cTzNR-ovg58ukRX6Jw2djYGF0uDjPoS8641ZCdWaw3k87cCw$ >

Software Engineer Intern – Python, Summer 2026 <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://grnh.se/4gqo8lht1us__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!WQiQ2jPYTiz-0cuwrCAyXyhJCdT-xBXzxmSs_RKwxX9yukvqI33qK7cTzNR-ovg58ukRX6Jw2djYGF0uDjPoS8641ZCdWaxGfSCa-g$ >

*Summer 2026 at Build for Good Labs!*









Interested in building with AI + technology to support meaningful social
impact? [image: 🚀]


Build For Good Labs is recruiting students for our 2026 Summer Student
Builder Program: a free hands-on, remote experience where you’ll design,
build, and ship a real product addressing a real-world challenge. You’ll
collaborate in small teams with industry mentors, explore problem spaces
thoughtfully, and use modern tools to turn ideas into working solutions.
This program is about learning by building, gaining real experience, and
contributing to work that matters.

No perfect resume required. Curiosity, commitment, and a willingness to
learn go a long way.

Learn more + apply HERE
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.buildforgoodlabs.org/2026-student-builder-program__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!WQiQ2jPYTiz-0cuwrCAyXyhJCdT-xBXzxmSs_RKwxX9yukvqI33qK7cTzNR-ovg58ukRX6Jw2djYGF0uDjPoS8641ZCdWawjjxn4Rw$ >!


*D. E. Shaw Group — Summer 2027 Internships*










The D. E. Shaw group is now accepting applications for Summer 2027
internships in New York, with roles across investing, trading, research,
and strategy & business development. Opportunities are open to students
from all academic backgrounds interested in technology, finance, and
problem-solving.


Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, and interviews will begin
soon. Notably, offers do not have time limits. Additional teams and
international offices (London, Hong Kong, Singapore) will open applications
later this year.


If you’re interested in learning more or applying, check the D. E. Shaw
group’s careers site HERE <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.deshaw.com/careers/internships__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!WQiQ2jPYTiz-0cuwrCAyXyhJCdT-xBXzxmSs_RKwxX9yukvqI33qK7cTzNR-ovg58ukRX6Jw2djYGF0uDjPoS8641ZCdWax4LLxfFg$ >!




*Trading Desk Operations Engineer (TDOE) 2026 Summer Internship*









Jane Street is currently looking for interns for Trading Desk Operations
Engineering roles! TDOEs
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.janestreet.com/join-jane-street/tdoe/__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!WQiQ2jPYTiz-0cuwrCAyXyhJCdT-xBXzxmSs_RKwxX9yukvqI33qK7cTzNR-ovg58ukRX6Jw2djYGF0uDjPoS8641ZCdWaySVI-f1Q$ > optimize
infrastructure, support trading systems, and resolve issues on our trading
desks—everything from querying data to designing tools that enhance desk
workflows.

Interns work on real projects that impact trading desks, guided by TDOE
mentors. They gain hands-on knowledge of trading products and develop
technical skills through tailored boot camps. Jane Street is looking for
individuals who are  passionate about optimization and finding solutions to
complex problems.

Learn more and APPLY HERE
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.janestreet.com/join-jane-street/position/8075334002/__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!WQiQ2jPYTiz-0cuwrCAyXyhJCdT-xBXzxmSs_RKwxX9yukvqI33qK7cTzNR-ovg58ukRX6Jw2djYGF0uDjPoS8641ZCdWayMdGs1GQ$ >!
*Applications
are evaluated on a rolling basis.*

*Summer 2026 Tech Internship Opportunities*

Looking for a tech internship for Summer 2026? This crowdsourced GitHub
repo  <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://github.com/vanshb03/Summer2026-Internships__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!WQiQ2jPYTiz-0cuwrCAyXyhJCdT-xBXzxmSs_RKwxX9yukvqI33qK7cTzNR-ovg58ukRX6Jw2djYGF0uDjPoS8641ZCdWaw_TP7Xvw$ >compiles a
regularly updated list of internship opportunities in software engineering,
data science, and related fields. A great resource to find and track open
positions!


*Other Resources:*

*WiC Alumni Database*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__http://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1iM1dULj3JIWcUlT5N_j1tXoU1ijYXph1JeNslt5zsMw/edit*gid=0__;Iw!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!WQiQ2jPYTiz-0cuwrCAyXyhJCdT-xBXzxmSs_RKwxX9yukvqI33qK7cTzNR-ovg58ukRX6Jw2djYGF0uDjPoS8641ZCdWaxpSrv7bA$ >

<http://mentor.northwestern.edu/ >

Join a course specific Slack Channel
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1uRLNdxU_LvaIbKDbSIJlz5GJK79Nuea-JsauzMlYwgw/edit*gid=0__;Iw!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!WQiQ2jPYTiz-0cuwrCAyXyhJCdT-xBXzxmSs_RKwxX9yukvqI33qK7cTzNR-ovg58ukRX6Jw2djYGF0uDjPoS8641ZCdWaxp5a1RVQ$ >

Give us feedback or ask us questions

subject: [POSTING] SOLIC Capital | Summer '26 and '27 Internships
body:
SOLIC Capital, a boutique investment bank focused on restructuring, is hiring.

They are looking for Juniors for their Summer 2026 internship program AND sophomores for their Summer 2027 internship program. Please see the attached job descriptions to learn more about the positions and application process.

More on SOLIC: SOLIC is building a premier business advisory and investment banking platform serving the middle market. Our team includes professionals with deep expertise across restructuring, financial advisory, investment banking, operations, and strategic consulting.

Position details:

  *   Perform industry and client research using internet searches, database subscriptions, and research reports

  *   Assist in compiling, editing, and proofing client presentations

  *   Support the creation and management of virtual data rooms

  *   Assist in drafting and editing Confidential Information Memorandums and teasers

  *   Participate in client due diligence meetings and buyer/investor presentations as requested

  *   Research prospective buyers / investors for investment banking engagements and track all solicitation processes

  *   Build and Develop valuation analyses and integrated financial models

     *   Three statement financial projections models

     *   Discounted cash flow with CapM cost of capital

     *   Transaction comparables

     *   Public trading comparables

     *   Liquidation analysis

     *   Leverage Buyouts/Recapitalization

     *   Merger Models

     *   Assist in development of 13-week cash flow forecasts

     *   Support preparation of bankruptcy related schedules and analyses

If you have any questions about the role, I would be happy to connect you with a Business Institutions alum who is working at SOLIC.

Best,
Meg


Meg Flanagan, M.Ed (She/Her)

Associate Director of Student Career Advising

Northwestern Career Advancement

Kapnick Center for Business Institutions

Schedule an appointment with me<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://app.joinhandshake.com/stu/appointments__;!!Dq0X2DkFhyF93HkjWTBQKhk!VNEy2Yx36t4ovEzW2uiLnVWVBWqCGjbKxtdm8xmY78_57qr07eUCZFmlGpZvVXhQQeyMOZPrI_Q1mDeas98U-zZqaayZ_g$ >

subject: Spoofing alert (graceshao2026@U.NORTHWESTERN.EDU)
body:
50  subscription   requests  have  been   received  in  the  last   4h  from
graceshao2026@U.NORTHWESTERN.EDU. A copy  of the mail headers  from the most
recent of  these requests  is enclosed below.  The subscription  requests in
question are now being cancelled.

Due to the protocols used on the Internet for mail delivery, it is extremely
easy for a  malicious user to forge an electronic  mail message from another
user. This vulnerability  has led to a practice known  as "spoofing" in list
owner jargon, through  which an innocent user is subscribed  to thousands of
mailing lists  with the intent of  filling up his mailbox  and rendering his
Internet account  essentially useless. This  is usually done  in retribution
for having  posted something on  a public  forum which made  the perpetrator
lose face.

There   is  unfortunately   no   defense  against   this  attack.   LISTSERV
automatically monitors  spoofing attempts  and blocks  subscription requests
after  a certain  threshold. In  addition,  it will  automatically undo  the
subscriptions which were accepted before  the spoofing attempt was detected.
Unfortunately, most  other mailing list  managers offer no  such protection,
and the victim must cancel the subscriptions individually.

An examination of the mail headers from the original request may reveal some
information about the  perpetrator. However, in most cases  this person will
be a  technically skilled  individual who  will know what  steps need  to be
taken in order  to hide one's track.  It is usually much  easier to identify
the perpetrator using traditional investigative methods, such as looking for
technically skilled individuals that the  victim has recently angered. It is
exceedingly rare, however, to find evidence that will stand in court.

subject: You are now subscribed to the ENGAGERP list
body:
Your are now subscribed to the ENGAGERP list (Engage RP).

Please save this message for future  reference, especially if this is the
first time you are subscribing to an electronic mailing list. If you ever
need to  leave the list,  then you  will find the  necessary instructions
below. Perhaps  more importantly, saving a  copy of this message  (and of
all future  subscription notices from  other mailing lists) in  a special
mail folder will give you instant access  to the list of mailing lists to
which you are subscribed.  This may prove useful the next  time you go on
vacation and need to leave the lists temporarily to avoid filling up your
mailbox while  you are away.  You should  also save the  welcome messages
from the  list owners  that you  may receive after  subscribing to  a new
list.

To send a message to all of  the people currently subscribed to the list,
just send mail to  ENGAGERP@LISTSERV.IT.NORTHWESTERN.EDU, which is called
the list address. You must never try to send any command to that address,
as it  would be distributed  to all the  people who have  subscribed. All
commands must be sent  to LISTSERV@LISTSERV.IT.NORTHWESTERN.EDU, which is
the  LISTSERV  address. It  is  important  to understand  the  difference
between the two.

You  may leave  the list  at  any time  by sending  a "SIGNOFF  ENGAGERP"
command  to  LISTSERV@LISTSERV.IT.NORTHWESTERN.EDU.  You  can  also  tell
LISTSERV how you want  it to confirm the receipt of  messages you send to
the list.  To send  yourself a  copy of  your own  messages, send  a "SET
ENGAGERP REPRO" command. Alternatively, to have LISTSERV send you a short

subject: You are now subscribed to the BADMINTON list
body:
Your are now subscribed to the BADMINTON list (BADMINTON).

Please save this message for future  reference, especially if this is the
first time you are subscribing to an electronic mailing list. If you ever
need to  leave the list,  then you  will find the  necessary instructions
below. Perhaps  more importantly, saving a  copy of this message  (and of
all future  subscription notices from  other mailing lists) in  a special
mail folder will give you instant access  to the list of mailing lists to
which you are subscribed.  This may prove useful the next  time you go on
vacation and need to leave the lists temporarily to avoid filling up your
mailbox while  you are away.  You should  also save the  welcome messages
from the  list owners  that you  may receive after  subscribing to  a new
list.

To send a message to all of  the people currently subscribed to the list,
just send mail to BADMINTON@LISTSERV.IT.NORTHWESTERN.EDU, which is called
the list address. You must never try to send any command to that address,
as it  would be distributed  to all the  people who have  subscribed. All
commands must be sent  to LISTSERV@LISTSERV.IT.NORTHWESTERN.EDU, which is
the  LISTSERV  address. It  is  important  to understand  the  difference
between the two.

You  may leave  the list  at any  time by  sending a  "SIGNOFF BADMINTON"
command  to  LISTSERV@LISTSERV.IT.NORTHWESTERN.EDU.  You  can  also  tell
LISTSERV how you want  it to confirm the receipt of  messages you send to
the list.  To send  yourself a  copy of  your own  messages, send  a "SET
BADMINTON  REPRO" command.  Alternatively, to  have LISTSERV  send you  a
short  acknowledgement  instead  of  the  entire  message,  send  a  "SET
BADMINTON   ACK   NOREPRO"   command.   Finally,   you   can   turn   off
acknowledgements  completely  with  the  "SET  BADMINTON  NOACK  NOREPRO"
command.

Note: For  users with a  valid Northwestern NetID,  subscription settings
and preferences can also be set using the NU Listserv Web Interface at at
https://listserv.it.northwestern.edu.  For  more   information  on  using
commands or on using the Web Interface to set your subscription settings,
see the List Subscriber's Manual at
http://www.lsoft.com/resources/manuals.asp.  For  users without  a  valid
Northwestern NetID, a  list of available e-mail commands can  be found at
www.it.northwestern.edu/listserv/howto.html .

Following  instructions from  the list  owner, your  subscription options
have been set  to "NOACK REPRO" rather than the  usual LISTSERV defaults.
For more information about subscription options, send a "QUERY BADMINTON"
command to LISTSERV@LISTSERV.IT.NORTHWESTERN.EDU.

Contributions  sent to  this  list are  automatically  archived. You  can
access the list archives at
https://LISTSERV.IT.NORTHWESTERN.EDU/cgi-bin/wa.exe?A0=BADMINTON. You may
need  to log  in or  get a  password for  the Web  interface to  view the
archives.

This  list is  available  in digest  form.  If you  wish  to receive  the
digested version  of the  postings, then issue  a "SET  BADMINTON DIGEST"
command.

More information on NU LISTSERV commands  can be found in the NU LISTSERV
reference  card, which  you can  retrieve  by sending  an "INFO  REFCARD"
command to LISTSERV@LISTSERV.IT.NORTHWESTERN.EDU.

subject: Plan Ahead: Holiday Schedule Updates Inside & More!
body: November 17, 2025   The end of the fall quarter is just around the corner, along with the hustle and bustle of the holiday season. As we head into this busy time of year, we want to share important schedule and program updates to help you plan ahead and make your holiday season a little easier.    Thanksgiving Break Hours With the Thanksgiving holiday approaching, our facilities and offices will operate on modified schedules from Saturday, November 22, through Sunday, November 30. Please review the individual schedules linked below, as hours vary by area.   FACILITY SCHEDULES Henry Crown Sports Pavilion  •  Norris Aquatics Center Blomquist Recreation Center  •  Membership Office  Winter Break Group Exercise Schedule Starting Monday, November 24, our Group Exercise program will transition to a condensed Winter Break schedule, which will run through the end of the break. *All classes are canceled November 27–28 (Thanksgiving) and December 24–January 1 (Winter Recess).   Winter Break Operations The Henry Crown Sports Pavilion and other recreational offices will transition to Winter Break hours starting Thursday, December 11. This includes the closing of Blomquist Recreation Center for the duration of the break, reopening on Monday, January 5, for Winter Quarter.   Winter Recess Pause As part of Northwestern’s annual Winter Recess, all recreational facilities will be closed, and all programming will be paused from December 24 – January 1. Winter Break operations will resume in the New Year on Friday, January 2      Unwind This Holiday Season Kick off the holiday season feeling your best with a relaxing massage from Northwestern Massage Services. Our certified therapists work with you to personalize your experience, leaving you feeling recharged and ready to tackle the holiday rush. Appointments are open to students, members, and non-members. Looking for a thoughtful gift idea? Give the gift of relaxation with a gift certificate!   Level Up Your BODYPUMP™ Journey Take your BODYPUMP™ passion to the next level! Join us January 10–11 at the Henry Crown Sports Pavilion for LES MILLS BODYPUMP™ Initial Training, a certification course that builds the technique and coaching skills to lead powerful, unforgettable classes. Club Sports Fall Highlights Women's Soccer capped off an incredible regular season, going 10–0.   Making program history, Women's Rugby had an undefeated regular season, earned a playoff berth, and climbed to #9 in the national rankings.   Sailing sailed into first place at their home regatta.   After a competitive season, Men’s Rugby qualified for a spot in the playoffs.   Tennis served up a win, taking first place at the Purdue Boiler Bash Tournament!     Save the Date! Get ready for another exciting summer with Northwestern Recreation! We’re thrilled to announce the registration dates for our 2026 summer camps and programs! Get ready for a summer packed with fun, learning, and unforgettable experiences. Be sure to mark your calendars and stay tuned for more details, including specific program offerings, dates, times, and more!   *Returning camper registration: Tuesday, January 20, 2026, at 10 a.m. *General camper registration: Monday, January 26, 2026, at 10 a.m. Adult Sailing & Windsurfing Classes: Monday, February 2, 2026 *Includes Wildcat J.V. and Varsity Sports Camps and Youth Sailing and Windsurfing Camps. Join Our Summer Camps & Programs Listserv Willie's Winter Wonderland is Back! Northwestern Athletics & Recreation is hosting seasonal favorite Willie's Winter Wonderland when the 'Cats take on Ohio State on Sunday, December 7. Enjoy hot chocolate, cookies, and winter-themed giveaways while supplies last! Enter code WWWB25 for discounted tickets! Click For Tickets   We're grateful you've chosen us to be part of your fitness and wellness journey. Wishing you a happy and healthy Thanksgiving!     Northwestern University Recreation | 2311 Campus Drive, Northwestern University Athletics and Recreation, Evanston, IL 60208 Unsubscribe Update Profile | Constant Contact Data Notice Sent by recmarketing@northwestern.edu powered by Try email marketing for free today!

subject: IMC First Meeting of the Quarter  –  Monday 1/12
body:
Hi everyone,

Welcome back! We hope you all had a restful and enjoyable holiday break.

We’re excited to kick off the quarter with our first meeting on Monday,
January 12. We’ll be jumpstarting the quarter with a speaker session
featuring Pranav Muthigi, a Kellogg MBA student and former Madison Dearborn
Partners, LLC professional.

We’re looking forward to a great conversation and hope to see everyone
there!

Best,
IMC Exec

subject: Invitation: Check Out the Off-Campus Life December Newsletter!
body:
Hello Wildcats,

We hope you had a restful Thanksgiving and got some time to eat well, relax, and be with friends and family. 🤎 We also hope the break helped you reset a bit — because the most exciting part is coming up: Winter Break is only about two weeks away, and Fall Quarter is almost over!

As we head into the final stretch, don’t forget to check out the Off-Campus Life newsletter<https://www.northwestern.edu/offcampus/resources/december-2025-ocl-newsletter.pdf >, now available on the OCL website. Go have a look! It’s the easiest way to stay connected while living off campus. If anything comes up — questions about housing, commuting, or off-campus life in general — don’t hesitate to reach out.

Good luck with exams, stay warm, and finish strong!

Best,
The OCL Team (Jason, Regina, Kylie, Camille, and Andrew)
